jobs.title: Compliance Associate
jobs.description:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 225 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 225 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide
advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

Lombard Odier Investment Managers (“LOIM”) is the asset management business of the Lombard Odier Group. In order to strengthen our Compliance Team, we are looking for a Luxembourg based:

Compliance Associate

YOUR ROLE

• Act as a key point of contact for compliance and AML (Anti-Money Laundering) matters within the organization.

• Support the implementation and ongoing enhancement of the compliance monitoring program, ensuring alignment with Luxembourg regulatory requirements (CSSF, AML/CFT, MiFID II, UCITS, AIFMD, etc.).

• Coordinate the migration of manual Compliance and AML processes (e.g., Excel-based) into automated Alteryx workflows and Tableau dashboards, driving digital transformation within the team.

• Collaborate with IT, business units, and other Compliance/AML team members to design and implement data-driven solutions that support decision-making and business tracking.

• Conduct compliance risk assessments, gap analyses, and thematic reviews.

• Monitor regulatory developments and assess their impact on business activities; ensure timely communication and implementation of new requirements.

• Draft, review, and update compliance policies, procedures, and internal controls.

• Support and coordinate regulatory reporting, internal/external audits, and respond to regulatory inquiries.

YOUR PROFILE

• University degree in Law, Finance, Business Administration, or a related field; additional compliance certifications (e.g., CAMS, ICA, or equivalent) are a strong asset.

• Relevant experience (up to 3 years) in a compliance, AML, or risk management role within the asset management or financial services industry, ideally in Luxembourg.

• Knowledge of Luxembourg and EU regulatory frameworks (CSSF, AML/CFT, MiFID II, UCITS, AIFMD, FATCA/CRS, etc.).

• Experience with Business Intelligence platforms (Tableau, Alteryx) and a strong interest in process automation and data analytics.

• Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and organizational skills; ability to manage mu

jobs.title: Juriste
Site Reliability Engineer (80-100%)
jobs.description:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 225 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

Site Reliability Engineer (80-100%)

As part of the modernization of our IT platform, we are looking for a Platform-oriented Site Reliability Engineer (SRE) who can support us in increasing our maturity around containers and the related ecosystem.

YOUR ROLE

• Ensure the reliability, scalability, and performance of the OpenShift platform and its ecosystem (Kong, Kafka, Vault).

• Manage the platform lifecycle: provisioning, updates, patching, and capacity planning.

• Implement and maintain observability solutions (metrics, logs, tracing) using Prometheus, Grafana, and associated tools.

• Establish governance for observability and operability of applications hosted on the platform.

• Handle incident response and perform root cause analysis for platform-related issues.

• Collaborate with security teams to apply best practices and ensure compliance in containerized environments.

• Contribute to the continuous improvement of the platform architecture and operational processes.

• Internal contacts : Databases, Systems, Telecom, Citrix, Development, Service Management (Incidents and Problems), Integration.

YOUR PROFILE

• Higher education in Computer Science (master’s degree or equivalent).

• 5 to 10 years of experience in a similar role

• Excellent command of OpenShift and/or Kubernetes administration

• Excellent command of monitoring and alerting systems (Prometheus, Grafana) and overall operational maintenance

• Excellent command of Linux (RHEL/CentOS)

• Excellent command of scripting (Shell, Python)

• Knowledge networking, security, and RBAC concepts in containerized environments

• Knowledge of high availability and disaster recovery strategies

• Responsible, rigorous, versatile, flexible, and with strong interpersonal skills. Team player, continuous improvement mindset, proactive

• Fluent in French and English

• Resident in Switzerland or willing to relocate

Our Maison’s DNA is defined by five core values. Excellence drives us to be the best at what we do, while Innovation fuels our progress. Respect underpins every interaction, and Integrity shapes our actions. Together, we are One Team , united in serving our clients with unwavering dedication.

As a responsible and supportive employer, we promote a diverse and inclusive work environment for our employees and candidates. Diversity, Equity and Inclusion are woven into the fabric of our Maison’s DNA, and we strive to ensure that our employees can fulfill both their personal and professional aspirations by encouraging internal mobility and individual upskilling programs. We firmly believe that building Diverse Teams contributes to our successes and to deliver on this, we actively embed Diversity, Equity and Inclusion in our business strategy.

It is an exciting time to join our Teams. All applications will be handled in the strictest confidence.

descriptionByLocale.it:
Una carriera a Lombard Odier significa lavorare per un rinomato asset manager globale, con un forte focus sugli investimenti sostenibili. Un'innovativa banca di scelta per clienti privati e istituzionali, il nostro Studio indipendente di proprietà è uno dei gruppi bancari più capitalizzati al mondo, che gestisce quasi 300 miliardi di franchi e opera da oltre 25 uffici in 4 continenti.

Con una storia di oltre 225 anni, Lombard Odier è una casa di investimento che offre un'offerta completa di gestione del portafoglio discrezionale e consultivo, servizi di ricchezza e custodia. Offriamo anche servizi di asset management e strategie di investimento attraverso Lombard Odier Investment Manager e fornire tecnologia bancaria avanzata ad altre istituzioni finanziarie.

“Rethink Everything” è la nostra filosofia – è al centro di tutto ciò che facciamo. Siamo cresciuti più forti attraverso più di 40 crisi finanziarie ripensando il mondo intorno a noi per fornire una nuova prospettiva di investimento per i nostri clienti.

Ingegnere di affidabilità del sito (80-100%)

Nell'ambito della modernizzazione della nostra piattaforma IT, siamo alla ricerca di un Ingegnere di Affidabilità del Sito (SRE) orientato alla Piattaforma che possa supportarci nell'aumentare la nostra maturità intorno ai container e all'ecosistema relativo.

Il tuo!

• Assicurare l'affidabilità, la scalabilità e le prestazioni della piattaforma OpenShift e del suo ecosistema (Kong, Kafka, Vault).

• Gestire il ciclo di vita della piattaforma: fornitura, aggiornamenti, patch e pianificazione della capacità.

• Implementare e mantenere le soluzioni di osservabilità (metriche, registri, tracciamento) utilizzando Prometheus, Grafana e strumenti associati.

• Stabilire la governance per l'osservanza e l'operabilità delle applicazioni ospitate sulla piattaforma.

• Mantenere la risposta agli incidenti e eseguire l'analisi delle cause root per problemi legati alla piattaforma.

• Collaborare con i team di sicurezza per applicare le migliori pratiche e garantire la conformità in ambienti containerizzati.

• Contribuire al miglioramento continuo dell'architettura della piattaforma e dei processi operativi.

• Contatti interni: Database, Sistemi, Telecom, Citrix, Development, Service Management (Incidenti e Problemi), Integrazione.

Il tuo progetto

• Istruzione superiore in Informatica (grado del maestro o equivalente).

• da 5 a 10 anni di esperienza in un ruolo simile

• Ottimo comando dell'amministrazione OpenShift e/o Kubernetes

• Comando eccellente dei sistemi di monitoraggio e allerta (Prometheus, Grafana) e manutenzione operativa complessiva

• Ottimo comando di Linux (RHEL/CentOS)

• Ottimo comando di scripting (Shell, Python)

• Rete di conoscenza, sicurezza e concetti RBAC in ambienti containerizzati

• Conoscenza delle strategie di alta disponibilità e di disaster recovery

• Responsabile, rigoroso, versatile, flessibile e con forti competenze interpersonali. Giocatore di squadra, mindset di miglioramento continuo, proattivo

• Fluente in francese e inglese

• Residente in Svizzera o disposto a trasferirsi

Il DNA della nostra Maison è definito da cinque valori fondamentali. L'eccellenza ci spinge ad essere i migliori a quello che facciamo, mentre l'innovazione alimenta i nostri progressi. Il rispetto sostiene ogni interazione, e l'integrità plasma le nostre azioni. Insieme, siamo un team unico, uniti nel servire i nostri clienti con dedizione inaspettata.

Come datore di lavoro responsabile e di supporto, promuoviamo un ambiente di lavoro diversificato e inclusivo per i nostri dipendenti e candidati. Diversità, equità e inclusione sono intrecciati nel tessuto del DNA della nostra Maison, e ci sforziamo di garantire che i nostri dipendenti possano soddisfare sia le loro aspirazioni personali che professionali incoraggiando la mobilità interna e programmi di upskilling individuali. Crediamo fermamente che la costruzione di Diverse Teams contribuisca ai nostri successi e a fornire su questo, abbiamo attivamente incorporato Diversità, Equità e Inclusione nella nostra strategia aziendale.

E' un momento emozionante per unirsi ai nostri team. Tutte le applicazioni saranno gestite nella massima fiducia.
descriptionByLocale.de:
Eine Karriere bei Lombard Odier bedeutet, für einen renommierten globalen Vermögens- und Vermögensverwalter mit einem starken Fokus auf nachhaltige Investitionen zu arbeiten. Eine innovative Bank der Wahl für private und institutionelle Kunden, unser unabhängiger Eigentümer Firm ist eine der besten Bankengruppen der Welt, die in der Nähe von CHF 300 Milliarden verwaltet und von über 25 Büros auf 4 Kontinenten.

Lombard Odier ist mit einer über 225 Jahre langen Geschichte ein Investmenthaus, das ein umfassendes Angebot an diskreten und beratenden Portfolio-Management, Vermögensdienstleistungen und Sorge bietet. Wir bieten auch Asset-Management-Dienste und Anlagestrategien durch Lombard Odier Investment Manager und bieten fortschrittliche Banktechnologie an andere Finanzinstitute.

„Rethink Everything“ ist unsere Philosophie – es liegt im Herzen von allem, was wir tun. Wir sind durch mehr als 40 Finanzkrisen stärker gewachsen, indem wir die Welt um uns herum neu denken, um unseren Kunden eine neue Investitionsperspektive zu bieten.

Site Reliability Engineer (80-100%)

Im Rahmen der Modernisierung unserer IT-Plattform suchen wir einen plattformorientierten Site Reliability Engineer (SRE), der uns bei der Steigerung unserer Reife um Container und das damit verbundene Ökosystem unterstützen kann.

IHRE ROLLE

• Gewährleistung der Zuverlässigkeit, Skalierbarkeit und Leistung der OpenShift-Plattform und ihres Ökosystems (Kong, Kafka, Vault).

• Verwalten des Plattform-Lebenszyklus: Bereitstellung, Updates, Patching und Kapazitätsplanung.

• Beobachtbarkeitslösungen (Metriken, Protokolle, Tracing) mit Prometheus, Grafana und zugehörigen Werkzeugen implementieren und pflegen.

• Stellen Sie Governance für die Einhaltung und Funktionsfähigkeit von auf der Plattform gehosteten Anwendungen fest.

• Handle-Crash-Antwort und führen Wurzel verursachen Analyse für plattformbezogene Probleme.

• Mit Sicherheitsteams zusammenarbeiten, um Best Practices anzuwenden und die Einhaltung in containerizzato Umgebungen zu gewährleisten.

• Beitrag zur kontinuierlichen Verbesserung der Plattformarchitektur und der operativen Prozesse.

• Interne Kontakte: Datenbanken, Systeme, Telecom, Citrix, Entwicklung, Service Management (Einfälle und Probleme), Integration.

IHRE PROFILE

• Hochschulbildung in Informatik (Master-Abschluss oder gleichwertig).

• 5 bis 10 Jahre Erfahrung in einer ähnlichen Rolle

• Ausgezeichneter Befehl von OpenShift und/oder Kubernetes Administration

• Hervorragender Befehl der Überwachungs- und Warnsysteme (Prometheus, Grafana) und der gesamten betrieblichen Wartung

• Ausgezeichneter Befehl von Linux (RHEL/CentOS)

• Hervorragender Befehl des Skripts (Shell, Python)

• Wissensvernetzungs-, Sicherheits- und RBAC-Konzepte in Containerumgebungen

• Wissen über hohe Verfügbarkeit und Katastrophenrückgewinnungsstrategien

• Verantwortungsvoll, streng, vielseitig, flexibel und mit starken zwischenmenschlichen Fähigkeiten. Teamplayer, kontinuierliche Verbesserung Mindset, proaktiv

• Fluent auf Französisch und Englisch

• Wohnsitz in der Schweiz oder bereit, sich zu verlagern

Die DNA unseres Hauses wird durch fünf Kernwerte definiert. Exzellenz treibt uns dazu, das Beste zu sein, während Innovation unseren Fortschritt vorantreibt. Respekt unterstützt jede Interaktion und Integrität prägt unsere Handlungen. Gemeinsam sind wir ein Team, vereint in der Betreuung unserer Kunden mit unerschütterlicher Hingabe.

Als verantwortungsbewusster und unterstützender Arbeitgeber fördern wir ein vielfältiges und inklusives Arbeitsumfeld für unsere Mitarbeiter und Kandidaten. Diversity, Equity und Inclusion sind in den Stoff unserer Maison’s DNA gewebt, und wir bemühen uns, sicherzustellen, dass unsere Mitarbeiter ihre persönlichen und beruflichen Bestrebungen erfüllen können, indem wir interne Mobilität und individuelle Upskilling-Programme fördern. Wir sind fest davon überzeugt, dass der Aufbau von Diverse Teams zu unseren Erfolgen beiträgt und dass wir darauf aktiv Diversity, Equity und Inclusion in unsere Geschäftsstrategie einbetten.

Es ist eine aufregende Zeit, sich unseren Teams anzuschließen. Alle Anwendungen werden im strengsten Vertrauen behandelt.
descriptionByLocale.fr:
Une carrière chez Lombard Odier signifie travailler pour un gérant mondial de patrimoine et d'actifs renommé, avec un accent fort sur l'investissement durable. Banque innovante de choix pour les clients privés et institutionnels, notre entreprise indépendante est l'un des groupes bancaires les mieux capitalisés au monde, gérant près de CHF 300 milliards et opérant depuis plus de 25 bureaux sur 4 continents.

Avec une histoire de plus de 225 ans, Lombard Odier est une maison d'investissement offrant une offre complète de gestion de portefeuille discrétionnaire et consultative, de services de patrimoine et de garde. Nous offrons également des services de gestion d'actifs et des stratégies d'investissement par l'intermédiaire de Lombard Odier Investment Managers et fournissons une technologie bancaire avancée à d'autres institutions financières.

Repensez Tout est notre philosophie – elle est au cœur de tout ce que nous faisons. Nous sommes devenus plus forts grâce à plus de 40 crises financières en repensant le monde qui nous entoure pour offrir une nouvelle perspective d'investissement à nos clients.

Ingénieur en fiabilité du site (80-100%)

Dans le cadre de la modernisation de notre plateforme informatique, nous sommes à la recherche d'un ingénieur de fiabilité du site orienté plate-forme (SRE) qui peut nous aider à accroître notre maturité autour des conteneurs et de l'écosystème connexe.

Votre rôle

• Assurer la fiabilité, l'évolutivité et la performance de la plateforme OpenShift et de son écosystème (Kong, Kafka, Vault).

• Gérer le cycle de vie de la plateforme : fourniture, mise à jour, patching et planification des capacités.

• Mettre en œuvre et maintenir des solutions d'observabilité (métriques, logs, traçage) en utilisant Prométheus, Grafana et les outils associés.

• Établir une gouvernance pour l'observation et le fonctionnement des applications hébergées sur la plateforme.

• Gérer la réponse incidente et effectuer une analyse des causes profondes des problèmes liés à la plateforme.

• Collaborer avec les équipes de sécurité pour appliquer les meilleures pratiques et assurer la conformité dans les environnements conteneurisés.

• Contribuer à l'amélioration continue de l'architecture de la plateforme et des processus opérationnels.

• Contacts internes : Bases de données, Systèmes, Télécommunications, Citrix, Développement, Gestion de Service (Incidents et Problèmes), Intégration.

VOTRE PROFIL

• Enseignement supérieur en informatique (maîtrise ou équivalent).

• 5 à 10 ans d'expérience dans un rôle similaire

• Excellente commande de l'administration OpenShift et/ou Kubernetes

• Excellente maîtrise des systèmes de surveillance et d'alerte (Prometheus, Grafana) et maintenance opérationnelle globale

• Excellente commande de Linux (RHEL/CentOS)

• Excellente commande de script (Shell, Python)

• La mise en réseau des connaissances, la sécurité et les concepts RBAC dans les environnements conteneurisés

• Connaissance des stratégies à haut niveau de disponibilité et de reprise après sinistre

• Responsable, rigoureux, polyvalent, flexible et avec de solides compétences interpersonnelles. Joueur d'équipe, amélioration continue de la mentalité, proactif

• En français et en anglais

• Résident en Suisse ou désireux de déménager

L'ADN de la Maison est défini par cinq valeurs fondamentales. L'excellence nous pousse à être les meilleurs dans ce que nous faisons, tandis que l'innovation alimente nos progrès. Le respect sous-tend chaque interaction, et l'intégrité façonne nos actions. Ensemble, nous sommes une équipe, unie pour servir nos clients avec un dévouement inébranlable.

En tant qu'employeur responsable et solidaire, nous favorisons un milieu de travail diversifié et inclusif pour nos employés et nos candidats. Diversité, équité et inclusion sont tissés dans le tissu de notre ADN Maison, et nous nous efforçons de faire en sorte que nos employés puissent réaliser leurs aspirations personnelles et professionnelles en encourageant la mobilité interne et les programmes de perfectionnement individuel. Nous croyons fermement que la création d'équipes diversifiées contribue à nos succès et pour y parvenir, nous intégrons activement la diversité, l'équité et l'inclusion dans notre stratégie d'affaires.

C'est un moment passionnant de rejoindre nos équipes. Toutes les demandes seront traitées avec la plus grande confiance.

jobs.title: Business Risk Manager (80-100%)
jobs.description:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 225 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

Business Risk Manager (80-100%) - LO Patrimonia

LO Patrimonia is an entity of Lombard Odier Group providing wealth planning and fiduciary services. With a team of forty experts whose skills cover Switzerland, the main European jurisdictions and other regions of the world, LO Patrimonia serves clients of the Lombard Odier Group exclusively. To cope with a growing and challenging regulatory environment, we are looking for a Risk and Control Officer.
The person will maintain an operational risk control plan and perform controls to ensure LO Patrimonia complies with its regulatory obligations and mitigates risks in accordance with Lombard Odier Group’s requirements. This includes the improvement of operational efficiency of tools and processes.
The person will work closely with LO Patrimonia’s management and legal/compliance responsible person as well as collaborate with other departments of Lombard Odier Group in the field of risk, operational and technology management.

YOUR MISSION

• Maintain LO Patrimonia’s risk control environment including governance, risk committee and reportings in accordance with Group Risk’s requirements

• Monitor activities and perform and/or consolidate controls for the risk committee

• Perform controls to monitor LO Patrimonia’s compliancy with regulatory obligations

• Act as data owner

• Act as Business Risk Manager and support LO Patrimonia’s management to comply with Lombard Odier Group Risk’ requirements

• Coordinate Internal Audit requests with relevant stakeholders and implement recommendations

• Manage the delivery of multiple projects requested by LO Patrimonia’s management or legal/compliance responsible person from initial analysis to implementation

• Collaborate to wider legal and regulatory projects initiated from other departments of the Lombard Odier Group and with the assistance of the department in charge of operations and organization

• Lead a variety of projects, ranging from electronic signature implementation to document management (GED) initiatives

YOUR PROFILE

• Minimum 5 years of relevant work experience in banking or fiduciary environment in a similar role or equivalent

• Good knowledge of regulatory requirements of Swiss Law, specifically FINMA’s circulars on AML and risk topics, corporate law, AMLA, FADP and cross-border rules (soft law)

• Good experience in the fields of compliance and risk researches and controls

• Knowledge of SQL language and overall understanding of IT hardware and software in order to be able to exchange with IT persons and popularize for non IT persons

• Proficient level with Microsoft office tools, especially Excel, Powerpoint, Adobe acrobat and ability to self-learn other applications

• Versatile, analytical, long term oriented, good interpersonal skills, willing to learn, tenacious, team player and communicator

• Fluent in French and English

• Resident in Switzerland

Our Maison’s DNA is defined by five core values. Excellence drives us to be the best at wh

jobs.title: Senior Compliance Assurance Officer (80-100%)

titleByLocale.it: Responsabile senior della garanzia della conformità (80-100%)
titleByLocale.fr: Agent principal d'assurance de la conformité (80-100%)

jobs.description:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 225 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

Senior Compliance Assurance Officer (80-100%)

Group Compliance Assurance is a dedicated team within Lombard Odier's Compliance department. The team based in Switzerland is responsible for implementing the Group Compliance Assurance Plan for our Swiss and foreign entities. The scope of the team's activities is in line with the mandate of the Compliance Department, focuses primarily on risks related to financial crime / sanctions and materially contributes to our Compliance Consolidated Supervision framework. The team's activities embed a forward-looking approach, with a view to translate the results of risk-based assurance activities into measurable improvements in the Compliance control environment.

YOUR ROLE

Based in Geneva and reporting to the Head of Group Compliance Assurance, you will assess (i) the design and operating effectiveness of relevant controls; (ii) compliance with applicable laws and regulations and adherence to our Compliance standards and internal policies for the Group Lombard Odier as follows:

• Develop the testing and review plans, including maintaining an inventory of test scripts.

• Perform and/or supervise testing and/or review activities

• Evaluate test and review results and communicate preliminary findings to stakeholders

• Document findings and their disposition as issues and actions in consultation with issue owners.

• Finalise and distribute reports to stakeholders.

• Monitor the completion of issues and actions through to issue closure for management reporting.

• Prepare and report progress status metrics as required

• Coordinate compliance assurance activities with other second line of defence units and Group Internal Audit

• Provide advice and guidance to the front office on actions to improve compliance in relation to the results of testing and reviews

• Propose and contribute to projects to continuously improve the control environment for compliance-related risks and other relevant cross-functional initiatives

• In accordance with Lombard Odier's professional standards, you will be expected to comply with all applicable legal, regulatory and internal compliance requirements, including but not limited to the Group Code of Conduct and Compliance Policies and Procedures. You will also be expected to maintain all professional certifications, as applicable, to be fully qualified to perform your role, including completing all mandatory training required to obtain and maintain such certifications.

YOUR PROFILE

Required qualifications/education

• Degree in law, economics, finance or a related field

• Additional professional certification in compliance an advantage
Experience/knowledge required

• Solid understanding of regulatory environments, particularly of the Swiss framework and the organization of consolidated supervision

• At least 10 years of relevant experience in financial services in the public or private sector, with particular exposure to private banking

• Experience in a compliance or audit ro

descriptionByLocale.it:
Una carriera a Lombard Odier significa lavorare per un rinomato asset manager globale, con un forte focus sugli investimenti sostenibili. Un'innovativa banca di scelta per clienti privati e istituzionali, il nostro Studio indipendente di proprietà è uno dei gruppi bancari più capitalizzati al mondo, che gestisce quasi 300 miliardi di franchi e opera da oltre 25 uffici in 4 continenti.

Con una storia di oltre 225 anni, Lombard Odier è una casa di investimento che offre un'offerta completa di gestione del portafoglio discrezionale e consultivo, servizi di ricchezza e custodia. Offriamo anche servizi di asset management e strategie di investimento attraverso Lombard Odier Investment Manager e fornire tecnologia bancaria avanzata ad altre istituzioni finanziarie.

“Rethink Everything” è la nostra filosofia – è al centro di tutto ciò che facciamo. Siamo cresciuti più forti attraverso più di 40 crisi finanziarie ripensando il mondo intorno a noi per fornire una nuova prospettiva di investimento per i nostri clienti.

Senior Compliance Assurance Officer (80-100%)

Group Compliance Assurance è un team dedicato all'interno del dipartimento di conformità di Lombard Odier. Il team con sede in Svizzera è responsabile dell'attuazione del Piano di Assicurazione Compliance per le nostre entità svizzere e straniere. La portata delle attività del team è in linea con il mandato del Dipartimento Compliance, si concentra principalmente sui rischi legati alla criminalità finanziaria / sanzioni e contribuisce materialmente al nostro quadro di Compliance Consolidated Supervision. Le attività del team hanno costituito un approccio previsionale, al fine di tradurre i risultati delle attività di assicurazione basate sul rischio in miglioramenti misurabili nell'ambiente di controllo Compliance.

Il tuo!

Con sede a Ginevra e segnalazione al Capo della Assicurazione Compliance di Gruppo, valuterai (i) la progettazione e l'efficacia operativa dei controlli pertinenti; (ii) il rispetto delle leggi e dei regolamenti applicabili e l'aderenza ai nostri standard di conformità e alle politiche interne del Gruppo Lombard Odier come segue:

• Sviluppare i piani di prova e di revisione, compreso il mantenimento di un inventario degli script di prova.

• Eseguire e/o supervisionare le attività di test e/o revisione

• Valutare i risultati del test e rivedere e comunicare i risultati preliminari agli stakeholder

• Risultati di documenti e loro disposizione come questioni e azioni in consultazione con i proprietari di emissione.

• Finalizzare e distribuire report alle parti interessate.

• Monitorare il completamento dei problemi e delle azioni attraverso la chiusura per la segnalazione della gestione.

• Preparare e segnalare le metriche di stato del progresso come richiesto

• Coordinare le attività di controllo della conformità con altre seconde unità di difesa e Gruppo Internal Audit

• Fornire consigli e indicazioni all'ufficio frontale sulle azioni per migliorare la conformità in relazione ai risultati dei test e delle recensioni

• Proporre e contribuire a progetti per migliorare continuamente l'ambiente di controllo per i rischi legati alla conformità e altre iniziative interfunzionali rilevanti

• In conformità con gli standard professionali di Lombard Odier, ci si aspetterà di rispettare tutti i requisiti di conformità legali, regolamentari e interni applicabili, inclusi, ma non limitati al Codice di condotta e di conformità del Gruppo. Si prevede inoltre di mantenere tutte le certificazioni professionali, come applicabile, per essere pienamente qualificati per svolgere il vostro ruolo, compreso il completamento di tutte le formazioni obbligatorie necessarie per ottenere e mantenere tali certificazioni.

Il tuo progetto

qualifiche richieste/istruzione

• Laurea in diritto, economia, finanza o settore relativo

• Certificazione professionale supplementare nel rispetto di un vantaggio
Esperienza / conoscenza necessaria

• Una solida comprensione degli ambienti normativi, in particolare del quadro svizzero e dell'organizzazione della vigilanza consolidata

• Almeno 10 anni di esperienza rilevante nei servizi finanziari nel settore pubblico o privato, con particolare esposizione al private banking

• Esperienza in conformità o audit ro
descriptionByLocale.de:
Eine Karriere bei Lombard Odier bedeutet, für einen renommierten globalen Vermögens- und Vermögensverwalter mit einem starken Fokus auf nachhaltige Investitionen zu arbeiten. Eine innovative Bank der Wahl für private und institutionelle Kunden, unser unabhängiger Eigentümer Firm ist eine der besten Bankengruppen der Welt, die in der Nähe von CHF 300 Milliarden verwaltet und von über 25 Büros auf 4 Kontinenten.

Lombard Odier ist mit einer über 225 Jahre langen Geschichte ein Investmenthaus, das ein umfassendes Angebot an diskreten und beratenden Portfolio-Management, Vermögensdienstleistungen und Sorge bietet. Wir bieten auch Asset-Management-Dienste und Anlagestrategien durch Lombard Odier Investment Manager und bieten fortschrittliche Banktechnologie an andere Finanzinstitute.

„Rethink Everything“ ist unsere Philosophie – es liegt im Herzen von allem, was wir tun. Wir sind durch mehr als 40 Finanzkrisen stärker gewachsen, indem wir die Welt um uns herum neu denken, um unseren Kunden eine neue Investitionsperspektive zu bieten.

Senior Compliance Assurance Officer (80-100%)

Group Compliance Assurance ist ein engagiertes Team innerhalb der Compliance-Abteilung von Lombard Odier. Das in der Schweiz ansässige Team ist für die Umsetzung des Group Compliance Assurance Plans für unsere Schweizer und ausländischen Unternehmen verantwortlich. Der Umfang der Aktivitäten des Teams entspricht dem Mandat der Compliance-Abteilung, konzentriert sich in erster Linie auf Risiken im Zusammenhang mit Finanzkriminalität / Sanktionen und trägt maßgeblich zu unserem Compliance-Konsolidierten Aufsichtsrahmen bei. Die Aktivitäten des Teams stellten einen zukunftsweisenden Ansatz dar, um die Ergebnisse von risikobasierten Sicherungsmaßnahmen in messbare Verbesserungen in der Compliance-Kontrollumgebung umzusetzen.

IHRE ROLLE

Mit Sitz in Genf und der Berichterstattung an den Konzernleiter Compliance Assurance bewerten Sie (i) die Gestaltung und operative Wirksamkeit relevanter Kontrollen; (ii) die Einhaltung geltender Gesetze und Vorschriften und Einhaltung unserer Compliance-Standards und internen Richtlinien für die Gruppe Lombard Odier wie folgt:

• Entwicklung der Test- und Überprüfungspläne, einschließlich der Aufrechterhaltung eines Inventars von Testskripten.

• Durchführung und/oder Überwachung von Prüf- und/oder Prüftätigkeiten

• Prüf- und Prüfergebnisse bewerten und vorläufige Befunde an die Beteiligten übermitteln

• Dokumentenbefunde und ihre Disposition als Themen und Aktionen in Abstimmung mit Emittenten.

• Abschluss und Verbreitung von Berichten an Interessenvertreter.

• Überwachen Sie die Fertigstellung von Problemen und Aktionen, um eine Schließung für die Management-Berichterstattung auszulösen.

• Bereiten und melden Sie die Fortschrittsstatus-Metriken nach Bedarf

• Koordinierte Compliance-Versicherungsaktivitäten mit anderen zweiten Verteidigungseinheiten und internem Audit der Gruppe

• Beratung und Beratung an der Frontstelle über Maßnahmen zur Verbesserung der Einhaltung der Ergebnisse von Tests und Überprüfungen

• Förderung von Projekten zur kontinuierlichen Verbesserung des Kontrollumfelds für konforme Risiken und andere relevante funktionsübergreifende Initiativen

• In Übereinstimmung mit den professionellen Standards von Lombard Odier wird erwartet, dass Sie alle geltenden gesetzlichen, regulatorischen und internen Compliance-Anforderungen einhalten, einschließlich, aber nicht beschränkt auf den Gruppenkodex für Verhaltens- und Compliance-Politiken und -Verfahren. Sie werden auch erwartet, dass alle professionellen Zertifizierungen, gegebenenfalls, vollständig qualifiziert sind, um Ihre Rolle zu erfüllen, einschließlich der Abschluss aller Pflichtschulungen erforderlich, um solche Zertifizierungen zu erhalten und zu pflegen.

IHRE PROFILE

Erforderliche Qualifikationen/Ausbildung

• Rechts-, Wirtschafts-, Finanz- oder verwandten Bereich

• Zusätzliche professionelle Zertifizierung in Übereinstimmung mit einem Vorteil
Erfahrung/Wissen erforderlich

• Solides Verständnis der regulatorischen Umgebungen, insbesondere des Schweizer Rahmens und der Organisation der konsolidierten Aufsicht

• Mindestens 10 Jahre relevante Erfahrungen im Bereich der Finanzdienstleistungen im öffentlichen oder privaten Sektor, insbesondere im privaten Bankwesen

• Erfahrung in einer Compliance oder Audit ro
descriptionByLocale.fr:
Une carrière chez Lombard Odier signifie travailler pour un gérant mondial de patrimoine et d'actifs renommé, avec un accent fort sur l'investissement durable. Banque innovante de choix pour les clients privés et institutionnels, notre entreprise indépendante est l'un des groupes bancaires les mieux capitalisés au monde, gérant près de CHF 300 milliards et opérant depuis plus de 25 bureaux sur 4 continents.

Avec une histoire de plus de 225 ans, Lombard Odier est une maison d'investissement offrant une offre complète de gestion de portefeuille discrétionnaire et consultative, de services de patrimoine et de garde. Nous offrons également des services de gestion d'actifs et des stratégies d'investissement par l'intermédiaire de Lombard Odier Investment Managers et fournissons une technologie bancaire avancée à d'autres institutions financières.

Repensez Tout est notre philosophie – elle est au cœur de tout ce que nous faisons. Nous sommes devenus plus forts grâce à plus de 40 crises financières en repensant le monde qui nous entoure pour offrir une nouvelle perspective d'investissement à nos clients.

Agent principal d'assurance de la conformité (80-100%)

Group Compliance Assurance est une équipe dédiée au département Conformité de Lombard Odier. L'équipe basée en Suisse est responsable de la mise en œuvre du plan d'assurance de la conformité du Groupe pour nos entités suisses et étrangères. La portée des activités de l'équipe est conforme au mandat du Département de la conformité, se concentre principalement sur les risques liés à la criminalité financière et aux sanctions et contribue de façon significative à notre cadre de surveillance consolidée de la conformité. Les activités de l'équipe intègrent une approche tournée vers l'avenir, en vue de traduire les résultats des activités d'assurance axées sur les risques en améliorations mesurables de l'environnement de contrôle de la conformité.

Votre rôle

Basé à Genève et relevant du Chef de Groupe Assurance de Conformité, vous évaluerez (i) la conception et l'efficacité opérationnelle des contrôles pertinents; (ii) le respect des lois et règlements applicables et le respect de nos normes de Conformité et politiques internes pour le Groupe Lombard Odier comme suit:

• Élaborer des plans d'essai et d'examen, y compris tenir un inventaire des scripts d'essai.

• Effectuer et/ou superviser des essais et/ou des examens

• Évaluer et examiner les résultats et communiquer les conclusions préliminaires aux parties prenantes

• Documenter les résultats et leur disposition en tant que questions et actions en consultation avec les propriétaires de questions.

• Finaliser et distribuer les rapports aux parties prenantes.

• Surveiller l'achèvement des problèmes et des actions en mettant fin aux rapports de gestion.

• Préparer et faire rapport sur l'état d'avancement des travaux au besoin;

• Coordonner les activités d'assurance de la conformité avec les autres unités de défense de deuxième ligne et l'audit interne du groupe

• Fournir des conseils et des conseils au front office sur les mesures à prendre pour améliorer la conformité aux résultats des essais et des examens;

• Proposer et contribuer à des projets visant à améliorer continuellement l'environnement de contrôle des risques liés à la conformité et à d'autres initiatives interfonctionnelles pertinentes;

• Conformément aux normes professionnelles de Lombard Odier, vous devrez respecter toutes les exigences légales, réglementaires et internes applicables en matière de conformité, y compris le code de conduite et les politiques et procédures de conformité du groupe. Vous devrez également conserver toutes les certifications professionnelles, selon le cas, pour être pleinement qualifié pour remplir votre rôle, y compris remplir toute la formation obligatoire requise pour obtenir et maintenir ces certifications.

VOTRE PROFIL

Diplômes/études requis

• Diplôme en droit, économie, finance ou domaine connexe

• Certification professionnelle supplémentaire en conformité un avantage
Expérience/connaissance requise

• Une bonne compréhension des environnements réglementaires, notamment du cadre suisse et de l'organisation d'une surveillance consolidée

• Au moins 10 ans d'expérience pertinente dans les services financiers dans le secteur public ou privé, avec une exposition particulière à la banque privée

• Expérience en matière de conformité ou d'audit

jobs.title: Senior Executive Assistant (70-100%)
Junior Change Manager (80-100%)
jobs.description:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 225 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

We are looking for a Junior Change Manager to assist the Head of PMO & Communication in supporting strategic initiatives and operational excellence across the CP COO Office.

YOUR ROLE

Change Management & Adoption

• Analyze change impacts and stakeholder readiness related to new tools, processes, or organizational changes.

• Define and execute change management plans (communication, training, engagement, adoption metrics).

• Act as a key liaison between business teams, IT, and project teams to ensure alignment and smooth change deployment.

• Identify resistance to change and proactively propose mitigation actions.

Communication & Content Management

• Contribute to the definition of editorial calendars for internal communication related to transformation initiatives.

• Ensure consistency of tone, branding, and messaging across all communication channels.

• Monitor and analyze communication effectiveness (open rates, engagement, feedback) and adjust strategies accordingly.

• Coordinate mass mailing campaigns and internal communications (newsletters, updates, invitations), ensuring timely delivery, consistency of messaging, and high quality proofreading.

Training & governance

• Design training strategies adapted to different user profiles and maturity levels.

• Coordinate the creation and maintenance of training materials and knowledge repositories.

• Produce AI‑generated training videos (e.g. tutorials, demos, explainers) to support scalable, engaging, and on‑demand learning.

• Track change-related KPIs (adoption rate, satisfaction, training completion, communication reach).

• Contribute to change management standards, templates, and best practices.

YOUR PROFILE

• University degree in Finance, Business Management, Engineering or related fields, or evidence of comparable academic / work achievement.

• 2-4 years relevant experience in Consulting in a PMO or Project Management Role with experience in financial services

• Able to prioritize in a fast moving, high pressure, constantly changing environment,

• Adaptability and resilience in a fast-paced, high-stakes banking environment.

• Strong analytical skills, the ability to summarize complex topics and convey key messages using proficiently MS Office tools such as PowerPoint, Excel

• Fluent in English and French; excellent communication (written and verbal), networking, influencing and presentation skills

Our Maison’s DNA is defined by five core values. Excellence drives us to be the best at what we do, while Innovation fuels our progress. Respect underpins every interaction, and Integrity shapes our actions. Together, we are One Team , united in serving our clients with unwavering dedication.

As a responsible and supportive employer, we promote a diverse and inclusive work environment for our employees and candidates. Diversity, Equity and Inclusion are woven into the fabric of our Maison’s DNA, and we strive to ensure that our employees can fulfill both their personal and professi

jobs.title: Private Assets Investment Analyst
jobs.description:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 225 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

Lombard Odier Investment Managers (“LOIM”) is the asset management business of the Lombard Odier Group. In order to strengthen our Private Assets department, we are looking for a Geneva based:

Investment Analyst- Private Assets

As an Investment Analyst, you will work closely with Investment Managers and broader team members across all stages of the private equity investment cycle, contributing to the sourcing, assessment, and monitoring of private asset opportunities

YOUR ROLE

• Maintain and update the private assets fund opportunity pipeline

• Analyse new private equity fund investments, co‑investments/directs, industry trends, and market dynamics

• Perform detailed due diligence on investment opportunities, including qualitative and quantitative analysis

• Prepare investment memoranda, due diligence documentation, and internal committee materials

• Monitor and report on existing fund investments, providing in‑depth performance insights

• Support the preparation of marketing materials for LOIM private equity strategies and products

• Develop sales pitches, presentations, and other materials for internal and external stakeholders

YOUR PROFILE

• Master’s degree, ideally in business, economics, finance, or engineering

• Prior professional experience in Private Equity, Corporate Finance, or Investment Banking is essential

• Native-level proficiency in English; professional working proficiency in French

• Strong analytical and problem‑solving skills, with excellent attention to detail

• Proactive, innovative, and results‑driven mindset

• Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, dynamic environment and manage multiple priorities

• Outstanding interpersonal and communication skills, with the ability to work both independently and collaboratively

Our Maison’s DNA is defined by five core values. Excellence drives us to be the best at what we do, while Innovation fuels our progress. Respect underpins every interaction, and Integrity shapes our actions. Together, we are One Team , united in serving our clients with unwavering dedication.

As a responsible and supportive employer, we promote a diverse and inclusive work environment for our employees and candidates. Diversity, Equity and Inclusion are woven into the fabric of our Maison’s DNA, and we strive to ensure that our employees can fulfill both their personal and professional aspirations by encouraging internal mobility and individual upskilling programs. We firmly believe that building Diverse Teams contributes to our successes and to deliver on this, we actively embed Diversity, Equity and Inclusion in our business strategy.

It is an exciting time to join our Teams. All applications will be handled in the strictest confidence.

descriptionByLocale.en:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 225 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

Lombard Odier Investment Managers (“LOIM”) is the asset management business of the Lombard Odier Group. In order to strengthen our Private Assets department, we are looking for a Geneva based:

Investment Analyst- Private Assets

As an Investment Analyst, you will work closely with Investment Managers and broader team members across all stages of the private equity investment cycle, contributing to the sourcing, assessment, and monitoring of private asset opportunities

YOUR ROLE

• Maintain and update the private assets fund opportunity pipeline

• Analyse new private equity fund investments, co‑investments/directs, industry trends, and market dynamics

• Perform detailed due diligence on investment opportunities, including qualitative and quantitative analysis

• Prepare investment memoranda, due diligence documentation, and internal committee materials

• Monitor and report on existing fund investments, providing in‑depth performance insights

• Support the preparation of marketing materials for LOIM private equity strategies and products

• Develop sales pitches, presentations, and other materials for internal and external stakeholders

YOUR PROFILE

• Master’s degree, ideally in business, economics, finance, or engineering

• Prior professional experience in Private Equity, Corporate Finance, or Investment Banking is essential

• Native-level proficiency in English; professional working proficiency in French

• Strong analytical and problem‑solving skills, with excellent attention to detail

• Proactive, innovative, and results‑driven mindset

• Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, dynamic environment and manage multiple priorities

• Outstanding interpersonal and communication skills, with the ability to work both independently and collaboratively

Our Maison’s DNA is defined by five core values. Excellence drives us to be the best at what we do, while Innovation fuels our progress. Respect underpins every interaction, and Integrity shapes our actions. Together, we are One Team , united in serving our clients with unwavering dedication.

It is an exciting time to join our Teams. All applications will be handled in the strictest confidence.
descriptionByLocale.it:
Una carriera a Lombard Odier significa lavorare per un rinomato asset manager globale, con un forte focus sugli investimenti sostenibili. Un'innovativa banca di scelta per clienti privati e istituzionali, il nostro Studio indipendente di proprietà è uno dei gruppi bancari più capitalizzati al mondo, che gestisce quasi 300 miliardi di franchi e opera da oltre 25 uffici in 4 continenti.

Con una storia di oltre 225 anni, Lombard Odier è una casa di investimento che offre un'offerta completa di gestione del portafoglio discrezionale e consultivo, servizi di ricchezza e custodia. Offriamo anche servizi di asset management e strategie di investimento attraverso Lombard Odier Investment Manager e fornire tecnologia bancaria avanzata ad altre istituzioni finanziarie.

“Rethink Everything” è la nostra filosofia – è al centro di tutto ciò che facciamo. Siamo cresciuti più forti attraverso più di 40 crisi finanziarie ripensando il mondo intorno a noi per fornire una nuova prospettiva di investimento per i nostri clienti.

Lombard Odier Investment Managers (“LOIM”) è l’attività di gestione patrimoniale del Gruppo Lombard Odier. Al fine di rafforzare il nostro dipartimento di beni privati, stiamo cercando una base di Ginevra:

Investimenti Analisti - Beni privati

In qualità di Investment Analyst, lavorerai a stretto contatto con Investment Manager e membri del team più ampi in tutte le fasi del ciclo di investimento azionario privato, contribuendo all'apprezzamento, alla valutazione e al monitoraggio delle opportunità di asset privati

Il tuo!

• Mantenere e aggiornare l'opportunitÃ del fondo patrimoniale privato

• Analizzare nuovi investimenti privati, co-investimenti/direzioni, tendenze del settore e dinamiche di mercato

• Eseguire la dovuta diligenza dettagliata sulle opportunità di investimento, tra cui l'analisi qualitativa e quantitativa

• Preparare la memoranda degli investimenti, la documentazione di due diligence e i materiali dei comitati interni

• Monitorare e segnalare gli investimenti dei fondi esistenti, fornendo approfondimenti sulle prestazioni

• Sostenere la preparazione di materiali di marketing per LOIM strategie private equity e prodotti

• Sviluppare campi di vendita, presentazioni e altri materiali per gli stakeholder interni ed esterni

Il tuo progetto

• Laurea magistrale, idealmente in economia, economia, finanza o ingegneria

• Prima esperienza professionale in Private Equity, Corporate Finance o Investment Banking è essenziale

• Competenza di livello nativo in inglese; competenza professionale in francese

• Forte capacità analitiche e di problem solving, con ottima attenzione ai dettagli

• Set mentale proattivo, innovativo e guidato dai risultati

• Capacità di prosperare in un ambiente dinamico e veloce e gestire più priorità

• Competenze interpersonali e di comunicazione eccezionali, con la capacità di lavorare in modo indipendente e collaborativo

Il DNA della nostra Maison è definito da cinque valori fondamentali. L'eccellenza ci spinge ad essere i migliori a quello che facciamo, mentre l'innovazione alimenta i nostri progressi. Il rispetto sostiene ogni interazione, e l'integrità plasma le nostre azioni. Insieme, siamo un team unico, uniti nel servire i nostri clienti con dedizione inaspettata.

Come datore di lavoro responsabile e di supporto, promuoviamo un ambiente di lavoro diversificato e inclusivo per i nostri dipendenti e candidati. Diversità, equità e inclusione sono intrecciati nel tessuto del DNA della nostra Maison, e ci sforziamo di garantire che i nostri dipendenti possano soddisfare sia le loro aspirazioni personali che professionali incoraggiando la mobilità interna e programmi di upskilling individuali. Crediamo fermamente che la costruzione di Diverse Teams contribuisca ai nostri successi e a fornire su questo, abbiamo attivamente incorporato Diversità, Equità e Inclusione nella nostra strategia aziendale.

E' un momento emozionante per unirsi ai nostri team. Tutte le applicazioni saranno gestite nella massima fiducia.
descriptionByLocale.de:
Eine Karriere bei Lombard Odier bedeutet, für einen renommierten globalen Vermögens- und Vermögensverwalter mit einem starken Fokus auf nachhaltige Investitionen zu arbeiten. Eine innovative Bank der Wahl für private und institutionelle Kunden, unser unabhängiger Eigentümer Firm ist eine der besten Bankengruppen der Welt, die in der Nähe von CHF 300 Milliarden verwaltet und von über 25 Büros auf 4 Kontinenten.

Lombard Odier ist mit einer über 225 Jahre langen Geschichte ein Investmenthaus, das ein umfassendes Angebot an diskreten und beratenden Portfolio-Management, Vermögensdienstleistungen und Sorge bietet. Wir bieten auch Asset-Management-Dienste und Anlagestrategien durch Lombard Odier Investment Manager und bieten fortschrittliche Banktechnologie an andere Finanzinstitute.

„Rethink Everything“ ist unsere Philosophie – es liegt im Herzen von allem, was wir tun. Wir sind durch mehr als 40 Finanzkrisen stärker gewachsen, indem wir die Welt um uns herum neu denken, um unseren Kunden eine neue Investitionsperspektive zu bieten.

Lombard Odier Investment Manager ("LOIM") ist das Asset Management-Geschäft der Lombard Odier Gruppe. Um unsere Private Assets-Abteilung zu stärken, suchen wir einen Genfer Standort:

Investment Analyst- Private Assets

Als Investment Analyst arbeiten Sie eng mit Investment Managern und weiteren Teammitgliedern in allen Phasen des Private-Equity-Investitionszyklus zusammen und tragen zur Beschaffung, Bewertung und Überwachung von privaten Asset-Möglichkeiten bei

IHRE ROLLE

• Erhaltung und Aktualisierung der Möglichkeiten des privaten Fonds

• Neue Private Equity-Fonds-Investitionen, Co‐Investments/Direkte, Branchentrends und Marktdynamik analysieren

• Durchführung detaillierter Due Diligence auf Investitionsmöglichkeiten, einschließlich qualitativer und quantitativer Analysen

• Vorbereitung von Investitionsgedächtnis, Due Diligence Dokumentation und interne Ausschussmaterialien

• Überwachen und Bericht über bestehende Fondsinvestitionen, die umfassende Leistungseinsichten bieten

• Unterstützung der Vorbereitung von Marketingmaterialien für LOIM Private Equity Strategien und Produkte

• Entwicklung von Verkaufsstellen, Präsentationen und anderen Materialien für interne und externe Akteure

IHRE PROFILE

• Masterstudium, ideal in Wirtschaft, Finanzen oder Ingenieurwesen

• Vorherige Berufserfahrung in Private Equity, Corporate Finance oder Investment Banking ist unerlässlich

• Native-Level-Proficiency in englischer Sprache; professionelle Arbeitsfähigkeit auf Französisch

• Starke analytische und problemlösende Fähigkeiten, mit ausgezeichneter Liebe zum Detail

• Proaktive, innovative und ergebnisorientierte Denkweise

• Fähigkeit, in einem schnelllebigen, dynamischen Umfeld zu leben und mehrere Prioritäten zu verwalten

• Herausragende zwischenmenschliche und kommunikative Fähigkeiten mit der Fähigkeit, unabhängig und kollaborativ zu arbeiten

Die DNA unseres Hauses wird durch fünf Kernwerte definiert. Exzellenz treibt uns dazu, das Beste zu sein, während Innovation unseren Fortschritt vorantreibt. Respekt unterstützt jede Interaktion und Integrität prägt unsere Handlungen. Gemeinsam sind wir ein Team, vereint in der Betreuung unserer Kunden mit unerschütterlicher Hingabe.

Als verantwortungsbewusster und unterstützender Arbeitgeber fördern wir ein vielfältiges und inklusives Arbeitsumfeld für unsere Mitarbeiter und Kandidaten. Diversity, Equity und Inclusion sind in den Stoff unserer Maison’s DNA gewebt, und wir bemühen uns, sicherzustellen, dass unsere Mitarbeiter ihre persönlichen und beruflichen Bestrebungen erfüllen können, indem wir interne Mobilität und individuelle Upskilling-Programme fördern. Wir sind fest davon überzeugt, dass der Aufbau von Diverse Teams zu unseren Erfolgen beiträgt und dass wir darauf aktiv Diversity, Equity und Inclusion in unsere Geschäftsstrategie einbetten.

Es ist eine aufregende Zeit, sich unseren Teams anzuschließen. Alle Anwendungen werden im strengsten Vertrauen behandelt.
descriptionByLocale.fr:
Une carrière chez Lombard Odier signifie travailler pour un gérant mondial de patrimoine et d'actifs renommé, avec un accent fort sur l'investissement durable. Banque innovante de choix pour les clients privés et institutionnels, notre entreprise indépendante est l'un des groupes bancaires les mieux capitalisés au monde, gérant près de CHF 300 milliards et opérant depuis plus de 25 bureaux sur 4 continents.

Avec une histoire de plus de 225 ans, Lombard Odier est une maison d'investissement offrant une offre complète de gestion de portefeuille discrétionnaire et consultative, de services de patrimoine et de garde. Nous offrons également des services de gestion d'actifs et des stratégies d'investissement par l'intermédiaire de Lombard Odier Investment Managers et fournissons une technologie bancaire avancée à d'autres institutions financières.

Repensez Tout est notre philosophie – elle est au cœur de tout ce que nous faisons. Nous sommes devenus plus forts grâce à plus de 40 crises financières en repensant le monde qui nous entoure pour offrir une nouvelle perspective d'investissement à nos clients.

Lombard Odier Investment Managers est une entreprise de gestion d'actifs du groupe Lombard Odier. Afin de renforcer notre département des actifs privés, nous recherchons une base Genève :

Analyste des investissements - Actifs privés

En tant qu'analyste des investissements, vous travaillerez en étroite collaboration avec les gestionnaires des investissements et les membres de l'équipe à toutes les étapes du cycle d'investissement en capital-investissement, contribuant ainsi à l'approvisionnement, à l'évaluation et au suivi des possibilités d'acquisition d'actifs privés

Votre rôle

• Maintenir et mettre à jour le portefeuille d'opportunités de fonds d'actifs privés

• Analyser les nouveaux investissements des fonds de capital-investissement, les co-investissements/directs, les tendances de l'industrie et la dynamique du marché

• Effectuer une diligence raisonnable détaillée sur les possibilités d'investissement, y compris une analyse qualitative et quantitative

• Préparer des mémorandums d'investissement, de la documentation sur la diligence raisonnable et du matériel de comité interne

• Surveiller les investissements existants et en rendre compte, en fournissant des informations approfondies sur le rendement

• Soutenir la préparation de matériel de marketing pour les stratégies et produits de fonds propres LOIM

• Développer des emplacements de vente, des présentations et d'autres matériaux pour les parties prenantes internes et externes

VOTRE PROFIL

• Maîtrise, idéalement en affaires, économie, finance ou ingénierie

• Une expérience professionnelle préalable en Private Equity, Corporate Finance ou Investment Banking est essentielle

• Compétences des autochtones en anglais; compétences professionnelles en français

• Fortes compétences d'analyse et de résolution de problèmes, avec une excellente attention aux détails

• Une mentalité proactive, innovante et axée sur les résultats

• Capacité de prospérer dans un environnement dynamique et rapide et de gérer de multiples priorités

• Compétences interpersonnelles et de communication exceptionnelles, avec la capacité de travailler de manière indépendante et en collaboration

L'ADN de la Maison est défini par cinq valeurs fondamentales. L'excellence nous pousse à être les meilleurs dans ce que nous faisons, tandis que l'innovation alimente nos progrès. Le respect sous-tend chaque interaction, et l'intégrité façonne nos actions. Ensemble, nous sommes une équipe, unie pour servir nos clients avec un dévouement inébranlable.

En tant qu'employeur responsable et solidaire, nous favorisons un milieu de travail diversifié et inclusif pour nos employés et nos candidats. Diversité, équité et inclusion sont tissés dans le tissu de notre ADN Maison, et nous nous efforçons de faire en sorte que nos employés puissent réaliser leurs aspirations personnelles et professionnelles en encourageant la mobilité interne et les programmes de perfectionnement individuel. Nous croyons fermement que la création d'équipes diversifiées contribue à nos succès et pour y parvenir, nous intégrons activement la diversité, l'équité et l'inclusion dans notre stratégie d'affaires.

C'est un moment passionnant de rejoindre nos équipes. Toutes les demandes seront traitées avec la plus grande confiance.

jobs.description:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 225 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

The Group Tax Department contributes to the value creation of the Group by controlling its tax. Joining the Group Tax Department as a Tax Intern in our Geneva headquarters, you will :

• Gain understanding of the daily work and organization of a Corporate Tax Affairs Department at the head office of a leading multinational group

• Obtain valuable professional experience whilst supporting the members of the Group Tax Department

YOUR ROLE

As an intern within our Group Tax Department, you will have the opportunity to participate in current projects and new initiatives. The role mainly consists in assisting all the team members in dealing with Swiss and International direct and indirect tax subjects.

Working closely with the entire tax team you may have the opportunity to perform the following tasks:

• Contribute actively to the internal domestic and international tax watch

• Conduct tax studies and legal researches to address concerns raised by the various stakeholders

• Participate in updating the transfer pricing documentation and tax disclosure/compliance obligations

• Assist in the preparation of tax computations and compliance work

• Support the team on indirect tax projects

• Assist when dealing with tax administrations’ requests

• Ad-hoc project work

YOUR PROFILE

• Strong interest for corporate taxation

• Master’s degree obtained (or in progress) in tax law, accounting or finance

• Fluent in French and English is necessary

• Good knowledge of Microsoft office suite (especially Excel and Word)

• Ambitious, enthusiastic, with a team oriented spirit and curious to learn through a first experience within a leading multinational group. You enjoy working closely and interact with colleagues within an international environment

• Available per July or September 2026

• Domicile in Switzerland

Our Maison’s DNA is defined by five core values. Excellence drives us to be the best at what we do, while Innovation fuels our progress. Respect underpins every interaction, and Integrity shapes our actions. Together, we are One Team , united in serving our clients with unwavering dedication.

As a responsible and supportive employer, we promote a diverse and inclusive work environment for our employees and candidates. Diversity, Equity and Inclusion are woven into the fabric of our Maison’s DNA, and we strive to ensure that our employees can fulfill both their personal and professional aspirations by encouraging internal mobility and individual upskilling programs. We firmly believe that building Diverse Teams contributes to our successes and to deliver on this, we actively embed Diversity, Equity and Inclusion in our business strategy.

It is an exciting time to join our Teams. All applications will be handled in the strictest confidence.

• Gain understanding of the daily work and organization of a Corporate Tax Affairs Department at the head office of a leading multinational group

• Obtain valuable professional experience whilst supporting the members of the Group Tax Department

YOUR ROLE

As an intern within our Group Tax Department, you will have the opportunity to participate in current projects and new initiatives. The role mainly consists in assisting all the team members in dealing with Swiss and International direct and indirect tax subjects.

Working closely with the entire tax team you may have the opportunity to perform the following tasks:

• Contribute actively to the internal domestic and international tax watch

• Conduct tax studies and legal researches to address concerns raised by the various stakeholders

• Participate in updating the transfer pricing documentation and tax disclosure/compliance obligations

• Assist in the preparation of tax computations and compliance work

• Support the team on indirect tax projects

• Assist when dealing with tax administrations’ requests

• Ad-hoc project work

YOUR PROFILE

• Strong interest for corporate taxation

• Master’s degree obtained (or in progress) in tax law, accounting or finance

• Fluent in French and English is necessary

• Good knowledge of Microsoft office suite (especially Excel and Word)

• Ambitious, enthusiastic, with a team oriented spirit and curious to learn through a first experience within a leading multinational group. You enjoy working closely and interact with colleagues within an international environment

• Available per July or September 2026

• Domicile in Switzerland

Our Maison’s DNA is defined by five core values. Excellence drives us to be the best at what we do, while Innovation fuels our progress. Respect underpins every interaction, and Integrity shapes our actions. Together, we are One Team , united in serving our clients with unwavering dedication.

As a responsible and supportive employer, we promote a diverse and inclusive work environment for our employees and candidates. Diversity, Equity and Inclusion are woven into the fabric of our Maison’s DNA, and we strive to ensure that our employees can fulfill both their personal and professional aspirations by encouraging internal mobility and individual upskilling programs. We firmly believe that building Diverse Teams contributes to our successes and to deliver on this, we actively embed Diversity, Equity and Inclusion in our business strategy.

It is an exciting time to join our Teams. All applications will be handled in the strictest confidence.

jobs.title: Développeur Full-stack IA/ML & Reporting Designer
jobs.description:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 225 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

Développeur Full-stack IA/ML & Reporting Designer

Rejoignez le département Technology & Operations de Lombard Odier au sein de l'équipe Content and Communications, responsable de la plateforme de gestion de contenu et de la production/distribution des documents clients et partenaires.

Nous recherchons un développeur Full-stack IA/ML et Reporting Designer afin de participer au développement de notre nouvelle plateforme technologique bancaire qui est également mise à disposition d’autres banques privées suisses et européennes.

Intégré(e) aux équipes produits, vous apporterez une expertise à l’intersection des LLM et de l’ingénierie logicielle appliquées à la finance : recherche documentaire augmentée (RAG), orchestration et optimisation d’inférence (on‑prem/cloud), intégration des modèles dans les parcours front‑office, et automatisation du reporting en production. Vous industrialiserez la génération et la personnalisation des documents via la suite Quadient Inspire (templates, composants, workflows et publication), en garantissant la qualité, la traçabilité et l’auditabilité de bout en bout. Vous évoluerez au sein d’une organisation agile, proche des utilisateurs finaux et du métier, au contact de technologies modernes et éprouvées.

VOTRE MISSION

• Contribuer aux développements de nos applications de gestion de contenu et de la production/distribution des documents.

• Comprendre les besoins des utilisateurs, les problèmes à résoudre ainsi que les contraintes d’ordre technique.

• Proposer et implémenter des solutions technologiques adaptées et tenant compte des aspects précités.

VOTRE PROFIL

• Master en informatique avec spécialisation en IA, Machine Learning et Data analytics suivi dans l'une des universités polytechnique suisse.

• Première expérience professionnelle ou stage significatif dans le développement applicatif, le reporting ou les outils documentaires.

• Computer Vision : Maîtrise des LLM/VLM appliqués aux documents (RAG, extraction visuelle, compréhension de mise en page).

• Expérience en détection d’anomalies documentaires (contrôles visuels, cohérence sémantique, règles métier).

• Maîtrise des langages de programmation Java et Python

• Expérience en LLM engineering : fine‑tuning, quantization, RAG (LangChain/LangGraph), et orchestration (LlamaIndex).

• Connaissance du framework de développement d’application web Angular

• Expérience conteneurs (Dockers / Kubernetes) et DevOps

• Expérience pratique des méthodologies Agile, Scrum est un plus

• Maîtrise courante du français et de l’anglais

• Vous avez une excellente communication à l’écrit comme à l’oral

• Vous avez un bon esprit d’analyse et une bonne capacité d'abstraction afin d’identifier et expliciter le problème à résoudre avant de penser aux solutions

• Vous êtes capable de donner du feedback constructif et prêt à en recevoir

• Vous faites preuve d’une grande autonomie

• Domicilié en Suisse ou disposé à vous y établir

Our Maison’s DNA is defined by five core valu
