jobs.title: Senior Accountant
jobs.description:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 230 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

Senior Accountant (100%)

Vous serez amené(e) à prendre en charge différentes activités en lien avec des clients de la Banque Lombard Odier, à entretenir un contact permanent avec ces derniers et à collaborer avec les autres services de LO Patrimonia et de la Banque.

VOTRE MISSION :

• Tenue des comptabilités de sociétés (principalement de détentions d’actifs), fondations (principalement caritatives)

• Accompagnement et suivi régulier d’un client en particulier

• Passation d’écritures comptables (y compris le bouclement)

• Etablissement des documents de reporting nécessaires

• Suivi administratif lié aux tâches de comptabilité

• Contacts avec les réviseurs

• Participation à la gestion et au développement des outils informatiques comptables

VOTRE PROFIL :

• Très bonnes connaissances comptables (règles comptables suisses et internationales-IFRS).

• Expérience professionnelle de minimum 10 années dans le domaine bancaire et/ou fiduciaire.

• Connaissance du fonctionnement des caisses de pension un atout.

• A l’aise avec des systèmes informatiques multiples, maîtrisant notamment Excel, fonctions et macros avancées.

• Notions de gestion des salaires un atout.

• Très bonnes connaissances de l'anglais.

• Esprit d’équipe, autonome, flexible, précision et sens du détail.

• Sens des responsabilités, entregent et bonne capacité relationnelle.

• Intérêt et facilité pour les contacts avec les différents intervenants (conseillers juridiques et fiscaux, gérants des entités, banquiers).

• Domicile en Suisse.

Our Maison’s DNA is defined by five core values. Excellence drives us to be the best at what we do, while Innovation fuels our progress. Respect underpins every interaction, and Integrity shapes our actions. Together, we are One Team , united in serving our clients with unwavering dedication.

As a responsible and supportive employer, we promote a diverse and inclusive work environment for our employees and candidates. Diversity, Equity and Inclusion are woven into the fabric of our Maison’s DNA, and we strive to ensure that our employees can fulfill both their personal and professional aspirations by encouraging internal mobility and individual upskilling programs. We firmly believe that building Diverse Teams contributes to our successes and to deliver on this, we actively embed Diversity, Equity and Inclusion in our business strategy.

It is an exciting time to join our Teams. All applications will be handled in the strictest confidence.

• Très bonnes connaissances comptables (règles comptables suisses et internationales-IFRS).

• Expérience professionnelle de minimum 10 années dans le domaine bancaire et/ou fiduciaire.

• Connaissance du fonctionnement des caisses de pension un atout.

• A l’aise avec des systèmes informatiques multiples, maîtrisant notamment Excel, fonctions et macros avancées.

• Notions de gestion des salaires un atout.

• Très bonnes connaissances de l'anglais.

• Esprit d’équipe, autonome, flexible, précision et sens du détail.

• Sens des responsabilités, entregent et bonne capacité relationnelle.

• Intérêt et facilité pour les contacts avec les différents intervenants (conseillers juridiques et fiscaux, gérants des entités, banquiers).

• Domicile en Suisse.

Our Maison’s DNA is defined by five core values. Excellence drives us to be the best at what we do, while Innovation fuels our progress. Respect underpins every interaction, and Integrity shapes our actions. Together, we are One Team , united in serving our clients with unwavering dedication.

As a responsible and supportive employer, we promote a diverse and inclusive work environment for our employees and candidates. Diversity, Equity and Inclusion are woven into the fabric of our Maison’s DNA, and we strive to ensure that our employees can fulfill both their personal and professional aspirations by encouraging internal mobility and individual upskilling programs. We firmly believe that building Diverse Teams contributes to our successes and to deliver on this, we actively embed Diversity, Equity and Inclusion in our business strategy.

It is an exciting time to join our Teams. All applications will be handled in the strictest confidence.

jobs.title: Compensation and Benefits Specialist (9-month contract)
Head of IT Security Operations & Controls (80-100%)

titleByLocale.en: Head of IT Security Operations & Controls (80-100%)

jobs.description:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 230 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

Head of IT Security Operations & Controls (80-100%)

The CISO organization protects Lombard Odier and our Clients against the risk of data theft and business disruption due to cyber-attacks or malicious insiders. The team is composed of approx. 15 talented team members that cover all aspects of security for the Group.

Reporting to our Chief Information Security Officer, you will lead the bank’s Operational Security function - owning our SoC (analysts and engineering), incident response, and the effectiveness of our frontline security controls. Your mission is to maximize the safe use of our digital assets, protect customers information, and keep the business fast, compliant, and resilient.

YOUR ROLE

• Manage a team of approx. 4 individuals: hiring, coaching, and creating an environment where people do the best work of their careers.

• SoC leadership & incident response: Run day‑to‑day SoC operations, elevate detection & response maturity, and lead major incident command calmly under pressure. Ensure investigations are rigorous, evidence‑based, and drive tactical fixes as well as strategic improvements.

• Strategy & modernization: Define and execute the SoC strategy for the next 2–3 years, including pragmatic adoption of cloud and AI‑assisted threat detection, triage, and automation (SOAR).

• Security control operations: Own the operating effectiveness of key controls (e.g., vulnerability management, baseline security, DLP). Ensure continuous control monitoring, coverage metrics, and clear KRIs/KPIs that matter to the business.

• Establish, own, and execute the bank’s penetration testing, red team, and purple team roadmap -coordinating internal capabilities and external partners to simulate realistic adversary behaviors and ensure continuous improvement of detection, response, and control effectiveness.

• Oversee the operational workflow of the security team, including triaging incoming support tickets, security requests, and operational tasks. Ensure efficient dispatching and prioritization of work across the CISO organization and maintain clear service‑level expectations with internal stakeholders.

• Enterprise & engineering integration: Partner closely with Architecture, Engineering, Infrastructure, IT Ops, and Developers to land controls and patterns that scale.

• Resilience & recovery: Strengthen incident readiness, tabletop exercises, and post‑incident “close‑call” learning to boost resilience and reduce repeat issues.

• Stakeholder trust: Engage customers, auditors, and internal leaders; translate risk into clear business context; advocate for secure‑by‑default choices.

YOUR PROFILE

• 10+ years in cybersecurity with direct leadership of SoC and Incident Response functions, including people leadership (hiring, coaching, performance).

• Demonstrated success running security operations in complex environments (on‑premise and cloud)

• Strong systems thinking; you connect detections, controls, processes, and behaviors into a coherent

jobs.title: Junior HRIS Environment & Support Specialist (80-100%)
jobs.description:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 230 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

Junior HRIS Environment & Support Specialist (80-100%)

We are seeking a motivated Junior HRIS Environment & Support Specialist to join our HR Digital Transformation team. This role is ideal for someone with a strong IT foundation and a passion for supporting and improving HR systems. You will be trained in Workday and other technologies, and play a key role in maintaining and enhancing our HR digital ecosystem.
You’ll be part of a dynamic and collaborative team driving digital transformation in HR. The team vision is to enhance collaborators’ experience, achieve greater efficiency, and minimize risks.

YOUR ROLE

• Monitor and maintain the HR digital ecosystem to ensure stability and performance.

• Serve as the main point of contact for HRIS support and troubleshooting.

• Execute operational tasks such as monthly reporting and data quality checks.

• Implement solutions to reduce manual workload across HR teams.

• Ensure the run and maintenance of HR IT systems, including Workday and related tools.

• Maintain and monitor automated jobs and reports to ensure timely and accurate delivery.

• Manage and optimize HR-related data flows and database structures.

• Document HRIS procedures, workflows, and technical configurations to ensure knowledge sharing and continuity.

• Stay informed on emerging technologies, including AI, and contribute to the team’s technological watch.

YOUR PROFILE

• Master’s degree or Engineering degree in Information Technology or related field.

• More than 2 years of professional experience in IT or HRIS support.

• Excellent command of scripting (CMD batches) and Office 365 (particularly Excel)

• Good command of Windows Server administration

• Strong Interest in technological innovation and awareness of AI trends

• Strong problem-solving and analytical skills

• Proactive, adaptable, and service-oriented mindset

• Comfortable with change and continuous learning

• Autonomous and well-organized

• Excellent communication and interpersonal abilities

• Fluent in French and a good working level of English

• Residence in Switzerland or willingness to relocate

TECHNICAL ENVIRONMENT

• Windows Servers Administration basics

• Windows scripting (CMD batch)

• Microsoft Office Suite (advanced in Excel)

• Transact-SQL queries

• Advanced C# / .NET development skills

• Microsoft Visual Studio

• Version control tools (e.g. Git, SVN)

• Workday administration and integrations

• Microsoft SSIS (SQL Server Integration Services)

• Microsoft SSRS (SQL Server Reporting Services)

• Azure DevOps

• Agile methodologies

• PHP

• SQL Server Database Management

• Atlassian tools (Jira, Confluence)

Our Maison’s DNA is defined by five core values. Excellence drives us to be the best at what we do, while Innovation fuels our progress. Respect underpins every interaction, and Integrity shapes our actions. Together, we are One Team , united in serving our clients with unwavering dedication.

As a responsible and supportive employer, we

A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 230 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

Junior HRIS Environment & Support Specialist (80-100%)

We are seeking a motivated Junior HRIS Environment & Support Specialist to join our HR Digital Transformation team. This role is ideal for someone with a strong IT foundation and a passion for supporting and improving HR systems. You will be trained in Workday and other technologies, and play a key role in maintaining and enhancing our HR digital ecosystem.
You’ll be part of a dynamic and collaborative team driving digital transformation in HR. The team vision is to enhance collaborators’ experience, achieve greater efficiency, and minimize risks.

YOUR ROLE

• Monitor and maintain the HR digital ecosystem to ensure stability and performance.

• Serve as the main point of contact for HRIS support and troubleshooting.

• Execute operational tasks such as monthly reporting and data quality checks.

• Implement solutions to reduce manual workload across HR teams.

• Ensure the run and maintenance of HR IT systems, including Workday and related tools.

• Maintain and monitor automated jobs and reports to ensure timely and accurate delivery.

• Manage and optimize HR-related data flows and database structures.

• Document HRIS procedures, workflows, and technical configurations to ensure knowledge sharing and continuity.

• Stay informed on emerging technologies, including AI, and contribute to the team’s technological watch.

YOUR PROFILE

• Master’s degree or Engineering degree in Information Technology or related field.

• More than 2 years of professional experience in IT or HRIS support.

• Excellent command of scripting (CMD batches) and Office 365 (particularly Excel)

• Good command of Windows Server administration

• Strong Interest in technological innovation and awareness of AI trends

• Strong problem-solving and analytical skills

• Proactive, adaptable, and service-oriented mindset

• Comfortable with change and continuous learning

• Autonomous and well-organized

• Excellent communication and interpersonal abilities

• Fluent in French and a good working level of English

• Residence in Switzerland or willingness to relocate

TECHNICAL ENVIRONMENT

• Windows Servers Administration basics

• Windows scripting (CMD batch)

• Microsoft Office Suite (advanced in Excel)

• Transact-SQL queries

• Advanced C# / .NET development skills

• Microsoft Visual Studio

• Version control tools (e.g. Git, SVN)

• Workday administration and integrations

• Microsoft SSIS (SQL Server Integration Services)

• Microsoft SSRS (SQL Server Reporting Services)

• Azure DevOps

• Agile methodologies

• PHP

• SQL Server Database Management

• Atlassian tools (Jira, Confluence)

Our Maison’s DNA is defined by five core values. Excellence drives us to be the best at what we do, while Innovation fuels our progress. Respect underpins every interaction, and Integrity shapes our actions. Together, we are One Team , united in serving our clients with unwavering dedication.

As a responsible and supportive employer, we

titleByLocale.fr: Propriétaire du produit – Risque et conformité (Plateforme CRM)

jobs.description:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 230 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

Product Owner – Risk & Compliance (CRM Platform)

Lombard Odier Group operates its own proprietary banking technology platform used by the Group and provided to other private banks and financial institutions through a BPO business model. The Technology & Operations (T&O) department maintains and develops the platform through the delivery of solutions for the Bank’s business divisions.

Within Lombard Odier’s Development department, we seek to strengthen our teams with an experienced Product Owner dedicated to the Risk & Compliance domain within our Customer Relationship Management (CRM) product. Our bankers’ CRM—primarily developed in-house—is fully integrated within our Wealth Management application suite and is a key differentiator of our technology offering. Given the central role of client lifecycle management and first-line controls in Private Banking, we aim to bring our CRM’s Risk & Compliance capabilities to the next level.

As a Product Owner for Risk & Compliance within the CRM platform, you will be part of the Wealth Management software development team and report to the CRM Product Manager. The team, working in an agile framework, brings together all “front-office” products serving bankers, relationship managers, clients, and key control functions. In your day-to-day work, you will collaborate with peer product owners, product managers, project managers, business analysts, developers, and—crucially—business stakeholders across Private Clients, Group Compliance & Risk, Tax, and Regulatory Reporting.

YOUR ROLE

• Define the product vision and roadmap for Risk & Compliance in CRM

• Own the end-to-end design and evolution of AML/KYC risk scoring within CRM

• Implement and streamline regulatory suitability & appropriateness processes

• Act as the primary interface between business stakeholders and the product team, ensuring clear understanding, timely feedback loops, and resolution of emerging needs and issues.

• Perform continuous market and regulatory watch on compliance tooling and operating practices (e.g., KYC/CLM, suitability, AML risk engines) to benchmark and anticipate changes.

• Proactively work with our 3rd party suppliers to drive value, establishing a solid and sound relationship.

• Lead potential RFI/RFP processes

• Foster a positive product team dynamic while maximizing ROI of product features as well as end users’ satisfaction.

• Maintain & prioritize the backlog by including changes and project demands while ensuring product maintenance to guarantee stability and sustainability over the long term.

• Set and communicate work priorities, updates and issues that emerge during the development process.

• Create sprint goals, actionable epics and user stories based on the requirements defined for each feature (function) in collaboration with the product team.

• Participate in project estimation costs based on business requirements.

• Ensure high product quality is met by overseeing test strategy definition, execution, and

descriptionByLocale.it:
Una carriera a Lombard Odier significa lavorare per un rinomato asset manager globale, con un forte focus sugli investimenti sostenibili. Un'innovativa banca di scelta per clienti privati e istituzionali, il nostro Studio indipendente di proprietà è uno dei gruppi bancari più capitalizzati al mondo, che gestisce quasi 300 miliardi di franchi e opera da oltre 25 uffici in 4 continenti.

Con una storia di oltre 230 anni, Lombard Odier è una casa di investimento che offre un'offerta completa di gestione del portafoglio discrezionale e consultivo, servizi di ricchezza e custodia. Offriamo anche servizi di asset management e strategie di investimento attraverso Lombard Odier Investment Manager e fornire tecnologia bancaria avanzata ad altre istituzioni finanziarie.

“Rethink Everything” è la nostra filosofia – è al centro di tutto ciò che facciamo. Siamo cresciuti più forti attraverso più di 40 crisi finanziarie ripensando il mondo intorno a noi per fornire una nuova prospettiva di investimento per i nostri clienti.

Proprietario del prodotto – Rischio e conformità (piattaforma CRM)

Lombard Odier Group gestisce la propria piattaforma di tecnologia bancaria proprietaria utilizzata dal Gruppo e fornita ad altre banche private e istituzioni finanziarie attraverso un modello di business BPO. Il dipartimento Technology & Operations (T&O) mantiene e sviluppa la piattaforma attraverso la fornitura di soluzioni per le divisioni aziendali della Banca.

All’interno del dipartimento Sviluppo di Lombard Odier, cerchiamo di rafforzare i nostri team con un esperto Product Owner dedicato al dominio Risk & Compliance all’interno del nostro prodotto Customer Relationship Management (CRM). Il CRM dei nostri banchieri, sviluppato principalmente in-house, è completamente integrato nella nostra suite di applicazioni Wealth Management ed è un differenziatore chiave della nostra offerta tecnologica. Data il ruolo centrale della gestione del ciclo di vita del cliente e dei controlli di prima linea in Private Banking, ci proponiamo di portare le capacità di Risk & Compliance del CRM al livello successivo.

In qualità di Product Owner for Risk & Compliance all'interno della piattaforma CRM, sarai parte del team di sviluppo software Wealth Management e riferisci al Product Manager CRM. Il team, lavorando in un quadro agile, riunisce tutti i prodotti “front-office” che servono banchieri, responsabili delle relazioni, clienti e funzioni di controllo chiave. Nel tuo lavoro quotidiano, collaborerai con i proprietari di prodotti peer, i product manager, i project manager, gli analisti aziendali, gli sviluppatori e, in particolare, le parti interessate di business tra clienti privati, Compliance & Risk, Tax e Regulatory Reporting.

Il tuo!

• Definire la visione del prodotto e la roadmap per Risk & Compliance in CRM

• Proprio il design end-to-end e l'evoluzione del rischio AML/KYC nel CRM

• Implementazione e semplificazione dei processi di idoneità normativa e idoneità

• Agire come interfaccia primaria tra gli stakeholder aziendali e il team di prodotto, assicurando una chiara comprensione, loop di feedback tempestivi e la risoluzione di bisogni e problemi emergenti.

• Eseguire il mercato continuo e l'orologio normativo sulle pratiche di conformità e di funzionamento (ad esempio, KYC/CLM, idoneità, motori di rischio AML) per benchmark e anticipare i cambiamenti.

• Proattivamente lavorare con i nostri fornitori di terze parti per guidare il valore, stabilendo una relazione solida e solida.

• Condurre potenziali processi RFI/RFP

• Promuovere una dinamica positiva del team di prodotti, massimizzando il ROI delle caratteristiche del prodotto e la soddisfazione degli utenti finali.

• Mantenere e privilegiare il backlog includendo modifiche e richieste di progetto, garantendo al tempo stesso la manutenzione dei prodotti per garantire stabilità e sostenibilità a lungo termine.

• Impostare e comunicare priorità di lavoro, aggiornamenti e problemi che emergono durante il processo di sviluppo.

• Crea obiettivi di sprint, epiche attuabili e storie utente basate sui requisiti definiti per ogni funzione (funzione) in collaborazione con il team del prodotto.

• Partecipare ai costi di stima del progetto in base ai requisiti aziendali.

• Assicurare un'alta qualità del prodotto è soddisfatta supervisionando la definizione di strategia di test, l'esecuzione e
descriptionByLocale.de:
Eine Karriere bei Lombard Odier bedeutet, für einen renommierten globalen Vermögens- und Vermögensverwalter mit einem starken Fokus auf nachhaltige Investitionen zu arbeiten. Eine innovative Bank der Wahl für private und institutionelle Kunden, unser unabhängiger Eigentümer Firm ist eine der besten Bankengruppen der Welt, die in der Nähe von CHF 300 Milliarden verwaltet und von über 25 Büros auf 4 Kontinenten.

Lombard Odier ist mit einer über 230-jährigen Geschichte ein Investmenthaus, das ein umfassendes Angebot an diskreten und beratenden Portfolio-Management, Vermögensdienstleistungen und Sorge bietet. Wir bieten auch Asset-Management-Dienste und Anlagestrategien durch Lombard Odier Investment Manager und bieten fortschrittliche Banktechnologie an andere Finanzinstitute.

„Rethink Everything“ ist unsere Philosophie – es liegt im Herzen von allem, was wir tun. Wir sind durch mehr als 40 Finanzkrisen stärker gewachsen, indem wir die Welt um uns herum neu denken, um unseren Kunden eine neue Investitionsperspektive zu bieten.

Product Owner – Risiko & Compliance (CRM Platform)

Die Lombard Odier Group betreibt eine eigene firmeneigene Banktechnologieplattform, die vom Konzern genutzt wird und anderen Privatbanken und Finanzinstituten über ein BPO-Geschäftsmodell zur Verfügung gestellt wird. Die Abteilung Technologie & Operations (T&O) unterhält und entwickelt die Plattform durch die Lieferung von Lösungen für die Geschäftsbereiche der Bank.

Innerhalb der Entwicklungsabteilung von Lombard Odier bemühen wir uns, unsere Teams mit einem erfahrenen Product Owner zu stärken, der sich der Risiko- und Compliance-Domain innerhalb unseres Produkts Customer Relationship Management (CRM) widmet. Unsere Bankers’ CRM – vor allem im Haus entwickelt – ist vollständig in unsere Anwendungssuite Wealth Management integriert und ist ein Schlüsseldifferenzierer unseres Technologieangebots. Angesichts der zentralen Rolle des Mandanten-Lebenszyklusmanagements und der erstklassigen Kontrollen in Private Banking wollen wir die Risiko- und Compliance-Fähigkeiten unserer CRM auf die nächste Stufe bringen.

Als Product Owner for Risk & Compliance innerhalb der CRM-Plattform werden Sie Teil des Wealth Management Software Development Teams sein und dem CRM Product Manager Bericht erstatten. Das Team, das in einem agilen Rahmen arbeitet, bringt alle “Frontoffice” Produkte zusammen, die Banker, Beziehungsmanager, Kunden und Schlüsselkontrollfunktionen bedienen. In Ihrer täglichen Arbeit werden Sie mit Peer Produktbesitzern, Produktmanagern, Projektmanagern, Business Analysten, Entwicklern und – geschäftsrelevanten Interessenvertretern über Privatkunden, Konzern Compliance & Risk, Tax und Regulatory Reporting zusammenarbeiten.

IHRE ROLLE

• Produktvision und Roadmap für Risiko & Compliance in CRM definieren

• Besitzen Sie die End-to-End-Design und Evolution von AML/KYC-Risiko-Scoring innerhalb von CRM

• Umsetzung und Optimierung der regulatorischen Eignungs- und Eignungsprozesse

• Aktivieren Sie als primäre Schnittstelle zwischen Geschäftspartnern und dem Produktteam, um ein klares Verständnis, zeitnahe Feedback-Schleifen und eine Lösung der aufstrebenden Bedürfnisse und Probleme zu gewährleisten.

• Führen Sie fortlaufende Markt- und Regulierungs-Uhren über Compliance-Tooling- und Betriebspraktiken (z.B. KYC/CLM, Eignung, AML-Risikomotoren) durch, um Änderungen zu bewerten und zu antizipieren.

• Proaktiv arbeiten mit unseren Anbietern der 3. Partei, um Wert zu steigern, eine solide und solide Beziehung aufzubauen.

• Lead Potential RFI/RFP Prozesse

• Foster eine positive Produkt-Team-Dynamik und Maximierung der ROI von Produktmerkmalen sowie der Zufriedenheit der Endbenutzer.

• Bewahren und priorisieren Sie den Backlog, indem Sie Änderungen und Projektanforderungen einschließen und gleichzeitig die Produktwartung sicherstellen, um Stabilität und Nachhaltigkeit langfristig zu gewährleisten.

• Stellen und kommunizieren Arbeitsprioritäten, Updates und Probleme, die während des Entwicklungsprozesses entstehen.

• Erstellen Sie Sprint-Ziele, actionable Epics und Benutzergeschichten basierend auf den für jede Funktion (Funktion) festgelegten Anforderungen in Zusammenarbeit mit dem Produktteam.

• Erwarten Sie die Kosten für die Projektschätzung auf der Grundlage der Geschäftsanforderungen.

• Gewährleistung hoher Produktqualität durch Überwachung der Definition von Teststrategien, Durchführung und
descriptionByLocale.fr:
Une carrière chez Lombard Odier signifie travailler pour un gérant mondial de patrimoine et d'actifs renommé, avec un accent fort sur l'investissement durable. Banque innovante de choix pour les clients privés et institutionnels, notre entreprise indépendante est l'un des groupes bancaires les mieux capitalisés au monde, gérant près de CHF 300 milliards et opérant depuis plus de 25 bureaux sur 4 continents.

Avec une histoire de plus de 230 ans, Lombard Odier est une maison d'investissement offrant une offre complète de gestion de portefeuille discrétionnaire et consultative, de services de patrimoine et de garde. Nous offrons également des services de gestion d'actifs et des stratégies d'investissement par l'intermédiaire de Lombard Odier Investment Managers et fournissons une technologie bancaire avancée à d'autres institutions financières.

Repensez Tout est notre philosophie – elle est au cœur de tout ce que nous faisons. Nous sommes devenus plus forts grâce à plus de 40 crises financières en repensant le monde qui nous entoure pour offrir une nouvelle perspective d'investissement à nos clients.

Propriétaire du produit – Risque et conformité (Plateforme CRM)

Le groupe Lombard Odier exploite sa propre plate-forme de technologie bancaire exclusive utilisée par le groupe et fournie à d'autres banques privées et institutions financières par le biais d'un modèle commercial BPO. Le département de la Technologie et des Opérations (T & O) maintient et développe la plateforme en fournissant des solutions aux divisions commerciales de la Banque.

Au sein du département Développement de Lombard Odier, nous cherchons à renforcer nos équipes avec un propriétaire de produit expérimenté dédié au domaine Risques et Conformité dans notre produit de gestion de la relation client (CRM). Nos banquiers, principalement développés à l'interne, sont pleinement intégrés dans notre suite d'applications Gestion de patrimoine et sont un différenciateur clé de notre offre technologique. Compte tenu du rôle central de la gestion du cycle de vie des clients et des contrôles de première ligne dans la banque privée, nous visons à porter nos capacités de risque et de conformité de CRM au niveau suivant.

En tant que propriétaire de produit pour le risque et la conformité au sein de la plate-forme CRM, vous ferez partie de l'équipe de développement de logiciel de gestion de patrimoine et rapporter au gestionnaire de produit CRM. L'équipe, travaillant dans un cadre agile, réunit tous les produits de Front-office pour les banquiers, les gestionnaires de relations, les clients et les fonctions clés de contrôle. Dans votre travail quotidien, vous collaborerez avec les propriétaires de produits par les pairs, les gestionnaires de produits, les gestionnaires de projets, les analystes d'affaires, les développeurs et, surtout, les intervenants d'affaires de la clientèle privée, la conformité de groupe et les risques, la fiscalité et les rapports réglementaires.

Votre rôle

• Définir la vision du produit et la feuille de route pour le risque et la conformité dans le CRM

• Posséder la conception de bout en bout et l'évolution de la notation des risques AML/KYC au sein du CRM

• Mettre en œuvre et rationaliser les processus d'adéquation et de pertinence réglementaires

• Faire office d'interface principale entre les parties prenantes et l'équipe produit, en assurant une compréhension claire, des boucles de rétroaction en temps opportun et la résolution des nouveaux besoins et enjeux.

• Effectuer une surveillance continue du marché et de la réglementation de l'outillage de conformité et des pratiques d'exploitation (p. ex. KYC/CLM, adéquation, moteurs à risque AML) pour évaluer et anticiper les changements.

• Travailler de manière proactive avec nos fournisseurs de troisième partie pour générer de la valeur, en établissant une relation solide et solide.

• Processus RFI/RFP potentiels de plomb

• Favoriser une dynamique d'équipe de produits positive tout en maximisant le ROI des caractéristiques du produit ainsi que la satisfaction des utilisateurs finaux.

• Maintenir et hiérarchiser l'arriéré en incluant les changements et les demandes de projets tout en assurant la maintenance des produits pour garantir la stabilité et la durabilité à long terme.

• Définir et communiquer les priorités de travail, les mises à jour et les questions qui se posent au cours du processus de développement.

• Créer des objectifs de sprint, des épopées actionnables et des histoires d'utilisateurs en fonction des exigences définies pour chaque fonction (fonction) en collaboration avec l'équipe produit.

• Participer à l'estimation des coûts du projet en fonction des besoins opérationnels.

• Veiller à ce que la qualité du produit soit élevée en supervisant la définition, l'exécution et la stratégie d'essai

jobs.title: Application Support Specialist - Front Office
jobs.description:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 230 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

Application Support Specialist - Front Office

We are looking for an Intern within the IT Operations department to provide application support for Front tools (Wealth Management) to the client-facing and operational units of Lombard Odier (Europe) S.A., as well as to our external partners.

YOUR ROLE

• Provide functional support for Wealth Management applications

• Identify, resolve, and troubleshoot all issues raised within the team

• Ensure maximum availability of applications and deliver quick solutions to Front teams’ queries

• Participate in quality checks and drafting of new application releases

• Train users on applications and assist our partners during UAT

YOUR PROFILE

• Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science or equivalent

• Strong interest in the banking sector

• Strong analytical and teamwork skills with a keen interest in client interaction

• Sense of responsibility

• Ability to work independently with minimal supervision

• Proficiency in French and English

• Resident in Switzerland or willingness to relocate

Our Maison’s DNA is defined by five core values. Excellence drives us to be the best at what we do, while Innovation fuels our progress. Respect underpins every interaction, and Integrity shapes our actions. Together, we are One Team , united in serving our clients with unwavering dedication.

As a responsible and supportive employer, we promote a diverse and inclusive work environment for our employees and candidates. Diversity, Equity and Inclusion are woven into the fabric of our Maison’s DNA, and we strive to ensure that our employees can fulfill both their personal and professional aspirations by encouraging internal mobility and individual upskilling programs. We firmly believe that building Diverse Teams contributes to our successes and to deliver on this, we actively embed Diversity, Equity and Inclusion in our business strategy.

It is an exciting time to join our Teams. All applications will be handled in the strictest confidence.

jobs.title: Business Application Support Specialist – Finance (80-100%)
jobs.description:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 230 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

Business Application Support Specialist – Finance (80-100%)

A Business Application Support Specialist plays a crucial role in ensuring the stability, availability, and optimal performance of banking applications. This role involves providing technical support, troubleshooting issues, and collaborating with various teams to maintain a reliable banking application environment. He is responsible for providing to the employees and to customers of Lombard Odier group with functional support applications in Finance domain. This support team’s main mission is the satisfaction and trust of colleagues and clients.

YOUR ROLE

• Identify, resolve and troubleshoot all Functional Support issues for finance and accounting applications.

• Participating in quality controls and documentation of new applications releases notes.

• Monitor KPIs to ensure SLAs are met and the service is on time and reliable.

• Escalate major incidents to the incident management team to ensure timely resolution.

• Organize and manage regular meetings with Bank partners for escalated cases, monitor closely the backlog of tickets

• Analysis of balance sheet in SAP.

• Ensure a quality training for bank users.

• Participate in new application release testing.

• Work closely with development and production teams.

• Ensure regular contact with our Bank partners Finance team.

• Actively contribute to enhance the day-to-day process of the team by improvement proposals to the head of Application Support

YOUR PROFILE

• Master’s degree in IT or Finance

• +5 years of experience in Banking

• Excellent communication and overall soft skills, proactivity, sense of initiative and sound experience in working under stress on unplanned matters

• Excellent command of banking, finance related operations and understanding of accounting principles

• Good command of Microsoft Office tools, specifically Excel & PowerPoint

• Previous experience as a Business Analyst or as an Auditor in the banking and financial sector is an advantage

• SAP BI knowledge is an advantage

• Residence in Switzerland or willingness to relocate

Our Maison’s DNA is defined by five core values. Excellence drives us to be the best at what we do, while Innovation fuels our progress. Respect underpins every interaction, and Integrity shapes our actions. Together, we are One Team , united in serving our clients with unwavering dedication.

jobs.title: Business Support Intern
jobs.description:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 230 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

Business Support Intern

Integrated to the Management Committee of Lombard Odier T&O Service (Europe) SA, as a Business Support Intern, you will gain hands-on experience in corporate governance and administrative support for executive leadership. This internship will provide exposure to legal frameworks, risk management, and decision-making processes within a corporate environment. Working closely with experienced professionals, you will develop essential skills in organization, communication, and confidentiality.

YOUR ROLE
- Coordination and preparation of presentation materials for the Management Committee (Townhall, ExCo, etc)
- Drafting & circulation of meeting minutes
- Preparation of Reporting such as Reg Reporting
- Management of common mailbox,
- Coordination of internal (sport committee, etc) & external events
- Procedures/Policies Management :

• Coordination with their respective managers regarding deadlines for updating procedures

• Coordination of the formal validation process

• · Management of publications on the Intranet and communication with LOT&OS Employees

YOUR PROFILE
As the sole person in this role, you will benefit from significant autonomy and the following qualities will be expected :
- Strong organizational and anticipation skills,
- Adaptibility,
- Strong interpersonal and diplomatic skills,
- High level of confidentiality,
- Strong writing skills in French and English
- Very good command of Words and Office package.

NB: Please be aware that in accordance with the current law and job requirements, the selected candidate will be requested to provide a criminal record (section 3).

Our Maison’s DNA is defined by five core values. Excellence drives us to be the best at what we do, while Innovation fuels our progress. Respect underpins every interaction, and Integrity shapes our actions. Together, we are One Team , united in serving our clients with unwavering dedication.

As a responsible and supportive employer, we promote a diverse and inclusive work environment for our employees and candidates. Diversity, Equity and Inclusion are woven into the fabric of our Maison’s DNA, and we strive to ensure that our employees can fulfill both their personal and professional aspirations by encouraging internal mobility and individual upskilling programs. We firmly believe that building Diverse Teams contributes to our successes and to deliver on this, we actively embed Diversity, Equity and Inclusion in our business strategy.

It is an exciting time to join our Teams. All applications will be handled in the strictest confidence.

A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. “Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

Business Support Intern

Integrated to the Management Committee of Lombard Odier T&O Service (Europe) SA, as a Business Support Intern, you will gain hands-on experience in corporate governance and administrative support for executive leadership. This internship will provide exposure to legal frameworks, risk management, and decision-making processes within a corporate environment. Working closely with experienced professionals, you will develop essential skills in organization, communication, and confidentiality.

YOUR ROLE
- Coordination and preparation of presentation materials for the Management Committee (Townhall, ExCo, etc)

- Preparation of Reporting such as Reg Reporting
- Management of common mailbox,
- Coordination of internal (sport committee, etc) & external events
- Procedures/Policies Management :

• Coordination with their respective managers regarding deadlines for updating procedures

• Coordination of the formal validation process

• · Management of publications on the Intranet and communication with LOT&OS Employees

YOUR PROFILE
As the sole person in this role, you will benefit from significant autonomy and the following qualities will be expected :
- Strong organizational and anticipation skills,
- Adaptibility,
- Strong interpersonal and diplomatic skills,
- High level of confidentiality,
- Strong writing skills in French and English
- Very good command of Words and Office package.

It is an exciting time to join our Teams. All applications will be handled in the strictest confidence.

jobs.title: Account und Relationship Manager - Global Custody und Private Label Funds Kunden (80-100%)

titleByLocale.de: Account und Relationship Manager – Global Custody und Private Label Funds Kunden (80-100 %)
titleByLocale.en: Account und Relationship Manager - Global Custody und Private Label Funds Kunden (80-100%)

jobs.description:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 230 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

Account und Relationship Manager - Global Custody und Private Label Funds Kunden (80-100%)

Als Teil eines kleinen, dynamischen und kundenorientierten Teams sind Sie verantwortlich für die umfassende Betreuung von strategisch wichtigen institutionellen Kunden in der Deutschschweiz.

Stellenbeschreibung:

• Umfassende Betreuung der Global Custody- und Private Label Funds Kunden (inkl. Trouble Shooting) in folgenden Bereichen: Depotbankdienstleistungen, Fondsbuchhaltung, Transfer Agent Aktivitäten, Bankinfrastruktur-Dienstleistungen, Business Process Outsourcing usw.

• Schnittstelle zwischen Kunden und diversen internen Stellen in Zürich, Genf und Luxembourg (Fondsleitung, Fondsbuchhaltung, Wertschriften-Operations, IT, Legal, Tax etc.) sowie Drittparteien (Vermögensverwalter, Revisoren, Consultants etc.)

• Eigenständige Führung von vielseitigen Kundenprojekten und Business Analyst Arbeiten (z.B. Lancierung von Anlagefonds, Offertstellung bei neuen Produkten, Service Reviews, technische Umsetzung von regulatorischen Anforderungen, Analyse von Kundenbedürfnissen etc.).

• Selbständige Vor- und Nachbearbeitung von Kundenmeetings (Lenkungsausschuss, operative Meetings etc.) und Führung / Teilnahme der Meetings

• Aufsetzen und Unterhalt von Verträgen und SLAs mit Kunden

• Erstellung von Kunden- und Gebührenabrechnungen und Ausführung von Gebührenchecks

• Eröffnung und Unterhalt der Kunden- und Kontenbeziehungen (Sicherstellung der korrekten Dokumentation, Aktualisierung der Kontodokumente und Verträge nach bankinternen Richtlinien etc.)

• Unterstützung bei der Ausführung von verschiedenen operativen Tätigkeiten wie Cash Management, Zahlungsverkehr, Abrechnung und Überwachung intern und extern gehandelter FX sowie Optionen- und Futures-Transaktionen. Koordination und Verbuchung von Transaktionen von nicht-deponierten Werten wie z.B. Private Equity, Infrastrukturanlagen, Immobilien etc.

• Mithilfe bei der Erstellung und Qualitätskontrolle von monatlichen und quartalsweisen kundenspezifischen Investmentreports (v.a. Fonds-Factsheets) für traditionelle und alternative Anlagen

Anforderungsprofil:

• Banklehre mit Berufsmaturität und abgeschlossene Weiterbildung (Bankwirtschafter/in HF, CFA /AZEK) oder Hochschulabschluss

• Mindestens fünf Jahre Berufserfahrung im Finanzsektor, idealerweise in der Betreuung von institutionellen Kunden

• Breite Fachkenntnisse im Bereich Asset Servicing (z. B. im Bereich Wertschriften, Börse, Back-& Middle-Office, Anlageberatung). Erfahrung im Kontakt mit externen Vermögensverwaltern und / oder Global Custody / Fonds-Kunden von Vorteil.

• Interesse am Fondsgeschäft, an operativen Abläufen (Prozessen) und Investmentthemen, verbunden mit Freude an Zahlen und regelmässigem Kontakt zu internen und externen Stellen

• Sehr gute MS Office Anwenderkenntnisse

• Kunden- und bedürfnisorientiert, hohes Qualitätsbewusstsein, genaue und selbstständige Arbeitsweise

• Teamfähige, engagierte und kommunikative Persönlichkeit mit hoher Soz

descriptionByLocale.it: Una carriera a Lombard Odier significa lavorare per una crescente ricchezza globale e asset manager, con un forte focus sugli investimenti sostenibili. Un'innovativa banca di scelta per clienti privati e istituzionali, il nostro Studio indipendente di proprietà è uno dei gruppi bancari più capitalizzati al mondo, che gestisce quasi 300 miliardi di franchi e opera da oltre 25 uffici in 4 continenti. Con una storia di oltre 230 anni, Lombard Odier è una casa di investimento che offre un'offerta completa di gestione del portafoglio discrezionale e consultivo, servizi di ricchezza e custodia. Offriamo quindi servizi di asset management e strategie di investimento attraverso Lombard Odier Investment Manager e fornire tecnologia bancaria avanzata ad altre istituzioni finanziarie. “Rethink Everything” è la nostra filosofia – è al centro di tutto ciò che facciamo. Siamo cresciuti attraverso più di 40 crisi finanziarie ripensando il mondo intorno a noi per fornire una nuova prospettiva di investimento per i nostri clienti. Account and Relationship Manager - Global Custody e Private Label Fund Clienti (80-100%) Come parte di un team piccolo, dinamico e orientato al cliente, siete responsabili del supporto completo di clienti istituzionali strategicamente importanti nella Svizzera tedesca. Descrizione del lavoro: • Supporto completo per clienti Global Custody e Private Label Fund (inclusi Trouble Shooting) nelle seguenti aree: Servizi bancari del deposito, Contabilità del fondo, Attività agente di trasferimento, Servizi di infrastruttura bancaria, Outsourcing del processo di affari ecc. • Interfaccia tra i clienti e varie agenzie interne a Zurigo, Ginevra e Lussemburgo (Fondsleitung, Fondsbuchhaltung, Wertschriften Legal-Operations. • Gestione di vari progetti di clienti e lavori di analisti aziendali (ad esempio lancio di fondi di investimento, offerta per nuovi prodotti, recensioni di servizi, implementazione tecnica dei requisiti normativi, analisi delle esigenze del cliente, ecc.). Sostegno finanziario e prenotazione di transazioni di valori non dichiarati come private equity, infrastrutture, immobili ecc. Esperienza a contatto con gli asset manager esterni e/o Global Custody / clienti di fondo di vantaggio. • Interessi nel settore dei fondi, nei processi operativi (processi) e nei problemi di investimento, combinati con la gioia dei numeri e il contatto regolare con gli enti interni ed esterni • Ottima conoscenza degli utenti di MS Office • Consapevolezza del cliente e della necessità, consapevolezza di alta qualità, lavoro accurato e indipendente • Personalità attiva, impegnata e comunicativa con soz elevato
descriptionByLocale.en:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a growing global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 230 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We therefore offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

Account and Relationship Manager - Global Custody and Private Label Funds Customers (80-100%)

As part of a small, dynamic and customer-oriented team, you are responsible for the comprehensive support of strategically important institutional customers in German Switzerland.

Job description:

• Comprehensive support for Global Custody and Private Label Funds customers (including Trouble Shooting) in the following areas: Depot Bank Services, Fund Accounting, Transfer Agent Activities, Bank Infrastructure Services, Business Process Outsourcing etc.

• Interface between customers and various internal agencies in Zurich, Geneva and Luxembourg (Fondsleitung, Fondsbuchhaltung, Wertschriften-Operations, IT, Legal, Tax etc.) and third parties (advisors, supervisors, consultants, etc.)

• Independent management of versatile customer projects and business analyst works (e.g. launching investment funds, offering new products, service reviews, technical implementation of regulatory requirements, analysis of customer needs etc.).

• Self-employed pre- and post-processing of customer meetings (learning committee, operational meetings etc.) and management / participation of meetings

• Set up and maintain contracts and SLAs with customers

• Creation of customer and fee bills and execution of fee checks

• Opening and maintenance of customer and account relations (sic production of correct documentation, updating account documents and contracts according to internal guidelines, etc.)

• Support in the execution of various operational activities such as cash management, payment transactions, billing and monitoring internally and externally traded FX and options and futures transactions. Coordination and booking of transactions of non-deposited values such as private equity, infrastructure, real estate etc.

• Using the creation and quality control of monthly and quarterly customer-specific investment reports (e.g. fund factsheets) for traditional and alternative investments

Requirements profile:

• Banking with professionalism and completed further training (bank economyr HF, CFA /AZEK) or university degree

• At least five years of professional experience in the financial sector, ideally in the support of institutional customers

• Wide expertise in asset servicing (e.g. in the field of securities, stock exchange, back-and- middle office, investment consulting). Experience in contact with external asset managers and/or Global Custody / fund customers of advantage.

• Interest in fund business, in operational processes (processes) and investment topics, combined with joy in numbers and regular contact with internal and external bodies

• Very good MS Office user knowledge

• customer and need-oriented, high quality awareness, accurate and independent working

• Team-ready, dedicated and communicative personality with high soz
descriptionByLocale.fr:
Une carrière chez Lombard Odier signifie travailler pour un gestionnaire mondial de patrimoine et d'actifs en croissance, avec un accent fort sur l'investissement durable. Banque innovante de choix pour les clients privés et institutionnels, notre entreprise indépendante est l'un des groupes bancaires les mieux capitalisés au monde, gérant près de CHF 300 milliards et opérant depuis plus de 25 bureaux sur 4 continents.

Avec une histoire de plus de 230 ans, Lombard Odier est une maison d'investissement offrant une offre complète de gestion de portefeuille discrétionnaire et consultative, de services de patrimoine et de garde. Nous offrons donc des services de gestion d'actifs et des stratégies d'investissement par l'intermédiaire de Lombard Odier Investment Managers et fournissons une technologie bancaire avancée à d'autres institutions financières.

Repensez Tout est notre philosophie – elle est au cœur de tout ce que nous faisons. Nous avons connu plus de 40 crises financières en repensant le monde qui nous entoure pour offrir une nouvelle perspective d'investissement à nos clients.

Gestionnaire de comptes et de relations - Clients des fonds de la Custodie mondiale et des fonds de la marque privée (80-100%)

Dans le cadre d'une petite équipe dynamique et orientée vers le client, vous êtes responsable du soutien complet de clients institutionnels d'importance stratégique en Suisse allemande.

Description du poste :

• Un soutien global aux clients des fonds de dépôt et des fonds de dépôt privés (y compris les opérations de tir) dans les domaines suivants : services bancaires de dépôt, comptabilité des fonds, activités des agents de transfert, services d'infrastructure bancaire, externalisation des processus d'affaires, etc.

• Interface entre les clients et diverses agences internes à Zurich, Genève et Luxembourg (Fondsleitung, Fondsbuchhaltung, Wertschriften-Operations, IT, Legal, Tax etc.) et des tiers (conseillers, superviseurs, consultants, etc.)

• Gestion indépendante des projets clients polyvalents et des travaux d'analystes d'entreprise (par exemple, lancement de fonds d'investissement, offre de nouveaux produits, revues de services, mise en œuvre technique des exigences réglementaires, analyse des besoins des clients, etc.).

• Pré-traitement et post-traitement des réunions des clients (comité d'apprentissage, réunions opérationnelles, etc.) et gestion/participation des réunions

• Établir et maintenir des contrats et des SLA avec les clients

• Création de factures de clients et de frais et exécution de contrôles de frais

• Ouverture et maintenance des relations client-compte (production de documents corrects, mise à jour des documents de compte et des contrats conformément aux directives internes, etc.)

• Appui à l'exécution de diverses activités opérationnelles telles que la gestion de la trésorerie, les opérations de paiement, la facturation et le suivi des opérations de change négociées à l'interne et à l'externe, ainsi que les options et les opérations à terme. Coordination et réservation des transactions portant sur des valeurs non déposées telles que les capitaux propres, les infrastructures, les biens immobiliers, etc.

• Utilisation de la création et du contrôle de la qualité de rapports mensuels et trimestriels sur les investissements spécifiques à la clientèle (par exemple fiches d'information sur les fonds) pour les investissements traditionnels et alternatifs

Profil des exigences:

• Banque avec professionnalisme et formation continue complète (banque économique HF, CFA /AZEK) ou diplôme universitaire

• Au moins cinq ans d'expérience professionnelle dans le secteur financier, idéalement dans le soutien des clients institutionnels

• Une vaste expertise dans le service des actifs (par exemple dans le domaine des valeurs mobilières, de la bourse, de l'administration centrale, de la consultation en investissement). Expérience en contact avec des gestionnaires d'actifs externes et/ou des clients d'avantage Global Custody / fonds.

• Intérêt dans les activités de fonds, dans les processus opérationnels (processus) et les thèmes d'investissement, associé à la joie en nombre et aux contacts réguliers avec les organismes internes et externes

• Très bonne connaissance des utilisateurs de MS Office

• sensibilisation du client et des besoins, haute qualité, travail précis et indépendant

• Personnalité d'équipe, dédiée et communicative avec haute soz

jobs.title: Equities & Derivatives Execution Trader (80-100%)

titleByLocale.it: Operatore di esecuzione di azioni e derivati (80-100%)

jobs.description:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 230 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

With over 250 professionals, our Investment Solutions department is responsible for providing sophisticated and highly customized investment proposals to our private clients.

Equities & Derivatives Execution Trader (80-100%)

Mission:

• Equities & Derivatives Trade Execution: Execute, monitor, and optimize the bank’s equity and derivative client flows across global markets, ensuring consistent best execution in line with applicable regulatory requirements and internal governance frameworks. The ideal candidate will have a strong expertise in future and option strategies).

• Late Shift: Take part in cross asset execution coverage until the U.S. market close (each execution trader participating in a limited number of late shifts per month)

• Technology & Automation: Actively Contribute to the enhancement of electronic trading strategies, leveraging trading data and analytics to help design implement, and scale new automation process

• Counterparty Interaction: Maintain strong relationships with brokers and market makers to optimize execution and access liquidity

Your Profile:

• Education: Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Finance, Economics, or a related field

• Experience: Minimum of 3 years in derivatives and equity trading

• Technical Skills: Proficiency in Bloomberg, TradeWeb and order management systems

• Familiarity with algorithmic and electronic trading solutions is highly advantageous

• Analytical & Execution Skills: Strong market analysis capabilities, sound judgment under pressure, and ability to handle high trade volumes with precision and efficiency

• Communication : Strong interpersonal skills with the ability to communicate clearly with senior management, counterparties, and technology teams

• Teamwork: Team-oriented mindset with the ability to collaborate effectively with investment professionals and clients

• Project skills: Proactive and detail-oriented, with a strong sense of initiative and active participation in cross-functional projects

• Languages: Fluent in both English and French

• Service minded, accurate, autonomous

• Must be a resident of Switzerland or willing to relocate

Our Maison’s DNA is defined by five core values. Excellence drives us to be the best at what we do, while Innovation fuels our progress. Respect underpins every interaction, and Integrity shapes our actions. Together, we are One Team , united in serving our clients with unwavering dedication.

As a responsible and supportive employer, we promote a diverse and inclusive work environment for our employees and candidates. Diversity, Equity and Inclusion are woven into the fabric of our Maison’s DNA, and we strive to ensure that our employees can fulfill both their personal and professional aspirations by encouraging internal mobility and individual upskilling programs. We firmly believe that building Diverse Teams contributes to our successes and to deliver on this, we actively embed Diversity, Equity and Inclusion in our business strategy.

It

jobs.title: Business Risk Manager - Technology & Operations (80-100%)

titleByLocale.it: Business Risk Manager - Tecnologia e operazioni (80-100%)

jobs.description:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 230 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

Business Risk Manager - Technology & Operations (80-100%)

As part of the Technology & Operations (T&O) Business Risk Management team, you will partner with stakeholders across Operations and Technology to identify key risks, coordinate remediation, and strengthen first‑line ownership of controls. You will contribute to the effective oversight of operational, technology and information risks, and ensure transparent and timely reporting to management.

YOUR ROLE

Risk Identification, Assessment & Reporting

• Identify, assess, and report operational risks, including processing, trade order execution, operational taxes, technology, cybersecurity, and information‑related risks across T&O.

• Support the RCSA process and ensure risk assessments remain current, well‑documented, and evidence‑based.

• Manage the T&O incident process, ensuring incidents are recorded, analysed, escalated, and followed through to closure.

Control Environment Oversight

• Contribute to the T&O Risk Assurance Plan through thematic reviews, independent design and operating effectiveness testing of the control environment, and follow‑up of identified gaps.

• Support the ISAE control framework, ensuring required evidence is collected, complete, and audit‑ready.

Remediation & Issue Management

• Drive, coordinate, and track risk remediation activities, ensuring actions are well defined, owned, and delivered on time.

• Monitor and report on the progression of identified issues, ensuring consistent closure, documentation, and controls uplift.

Governance, Assurance & Culture

• Promote a strong risk culture by supporting awareness, training, and constructive challenge across teams.

• Prepare structured and insightful risk reports to the T&O Risk Committee and senior management.

YOUR PROFILE

• Minimum 5 years' experience in risk management, internal control, audit, or consulting (e.g., Big Four) with focus on operational or technology‑related risks.

• Solid understanding of operations, IT, cybersecurity, and information risk, ideally within financial services.

• Strong analytical mindset with a structured, detail‑oriented approach.

• Excellent communication skills in English and French, with the ability to challenge stakeholders constructively.

• Resident in Switzerland or willingness to relocate

Our Maison’s DNA is defined by five core values. Excellence drives us to be the best at what we do, while Innovation fuels our progress. Respect underpins every interaction, and Integrity shapes our actions. Together, we are One Team , united in serving our clients with unwavering dedication.

As a responsible and supportive employer, we promote a diverse and inclusive work environment for our employees and candidates. Diversity, Equity and Inclusion are woven into the fabric of our Maison’s DNA, and we strive to ensure that our employees can fulfill both their personal and professional aspirations by encouraging internal mobility and individual upskilling programs. We firmly believe

• Identify, assess, and report operational risks, including processing, trade order execution, operational taxes, technology, cybersecurity, and information‑related risks across T&O.

• Support the RCSA process and ensure risk assessments remain current, well‑documented, and evidence‑based.

• Manage the T&O incident process, ensuring incidents are recorded, analysed, escalated, and followed through to closure.

Control Environment Oversight

• Contribute to the T&O Risk Assurance Plan through thematic reviews, independent design and operating effectiveness testing of the control environment, and follow‑up of identified gaps.

• Support the ISAE control framework, ensuring required evidence is collected, complete, and audit‑ready.

Remediation & Issue Management

• Drive, coordinate, and track risk remediation activities, ensuring actions are well defined, owned, and delivered on time.

• Monitor and report on the progression of identified issues, ensuring consistent closure, documentation, and controls uplift.

Governance, Assurance & Culture

• Promote a strong risk culture by supporting awareness, training, and constructive challenge across teams.

• Prepare structured and insightful risk reports to the T&O Risk Committee and senior management.

YOUR PROFILE

• Minimum 5 years' experience in risk management, internal control, audit, or consulting (e.g., Big Four) with focus on operational or technology‑related risks.

• Solid understanding of operations, IT, cybersecurity, and information risk, ideally within financial services.

• Strong analytical mindset with a structured, detail‑oriented approach.

• Excellent communication skills in English and French, with the ability to challenge stakeholders constructively.

• Resident in Switzerland or willingness to relocate

Our Maison’s DNA is defined by five core values. Excellence drives us to be the best at what we do, while Innovation fuels our progress. Respect underpins every interaction, and Integrity shapes our actions. Together, we are One Team , united in serving our clients with unwavering dedication.

As a responsible and supportive employer, we promote a diverse and inclusive work environment for our employees and candidates. Diversity, Equity and Inclusion are woven into the fabric of our Maison’s DNA, and we strive to ensure that our employees can fulfill both their personal and professional aspirations by encouraging internal mobility and individual upskilling programs. jobs.description:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 230 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

Stagiaire - Database Administration & Observability Specialist (Foglight / Elastic)

Dans le cadre du renforcement de la supervision des bases de données et du déploiement d’une plateforme d’observabilité centralisée, l’équipe DBA recherche un(e) stagiaire pour accompagner un projet stratégique autour de Foglight (monitoring bases de données) et de son intégration avec la plateforme Elastic utilisée à l’échelle de la banque.

Ce stage offre une exposition concrète à des environnements bancaires critiques , des technologies de supervision modernes et à un projet transverse impliquant plusieurs équipes IT (DBA, Systèmes, Sécurité, Observabilité).

Le/la stagiaire accompagnera l’équipe DBA dans le suivi, la coordination, le déploiement et la documentation du projet Foglight, ainsi que dans son intégration avec la plateforme d’observabilité Elastic .

Le stage combine :

• Pilotage fonctionnel de projet,

• Mise en œuvre technique encadrée,

• Communication et documentation,

• Découverte des bonnes pratiques de supervision en milieu bancaire.

VOTRE MISSION

1. Pilotage et coordination du projet Foglight

• Participer au suivi du planning global Foglight & Elastic .

• Organiser et coordonner les réunions avec :

• Les référents des différentes technologies DB au sein de l’équipe DBA,

• Les équipes Systèmes, Sécurité et Observabilité.

• Préparer les comptes-rendus , tableaux de suivi et reportings d’avancement.

• Contribuer à la circulation fluide de l’information entre les équipes.

2. Implémentation de la supervision Foglight

Accompagné(e) par les experts DBA, le/la stagiaire participera à :

• L’ajout des environnements Test / UAT / Production dans Foglight.

• La validation des métriques collectées pour les différents SGBD.

• La création de dashboards de santé haut niveau par technologie.

• La définition et le paramétrage des alertes pertinentes .

• L’intégration avec ServiceNow pour l’ouverture automatique d’incidents.

• L’intégration avec Elastic pour la remontée des métriques de supervision.

3. Documentation et structuration

• Rédiger et maintenir la documentation projet .

• Produire la documentation fonctionnelle de l’intégration Foglight → Elastic .

• Créer des guides utilisateurs et procédures internes destinés aux équipes IT.

4. Support à la formation interne

• Préparer des supports de formation (présentations, guides).

• Contribuer à l’organisation et au bon déroulement de sessions de formation internes sur Foglight et Elastic.

5. Communication et visibilité du projet

• Préparer les communications internes officielles liées au projet.

• Mettre à jour les espaces Confluence / Teams .

• Participer à la communication autour des sujets d’ observabilité au sein de la banque.

Missions spécifiques – Observabilité Elastic

• Recueillir et formaliser les besoins en métriques, alertes et dashboards Elastic.

• Décrire fonctionnellement les flux de données Foglight → Elastic .

• Coordonner les échanges entre l’équipe DBA et l’équipe O
